name: Yuddha Kanda
description: The great war between Rama and Ravana. Covers the building of the bridge to Lanka, the epic battles, the defeat of Ravana, and the coronation of Rama.

sargas.title: Sarga 1
sargas.introduction:
Rama appreciates Hanuma and embraces him after hearing his report.Rama was gladdend
that Hanuma did something worthy of a noble servent. Then, Rama reflected upon the problem of how
to cross the ocean.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रुत्वा हनुमतो वाक्यम् यथावद्भिभाषितम् |

रामः प्रीतिसमायुक्तो वाक्यमुत्तरमब्रवीत् || ६-१-१

कृतम् हनुमता कार्यम् सुमहद्भुवि दुर्लभम् |

मनसापि यदन्येन न शक्यम् धरणीतले || ६-१-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. sumahat = "a very outstanding; kaaryam = work;
durlabham = the most arduous; bhuvi = in the world; kR^itam = has been
done; hanumataa = by Hanuman; yat = which; na shakyam = could not be
achieved; manasaapi = even in thought; anyena = by another; dharaNiitale
= on the surface of this earth"
verses.translation:
"A very outstanding work, the most arduous in the world has been done by Hanuman,
which could not be carried out even in thought by any other on the surface of this earth."

verses.sanskrit:
न हि तम् परिपश्यामि यस्तरेत महोदधिम् |

अन्यत्र गुरुडाद्वायोरन्यत्र च हनूमतः || ६-१-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. na paripashyaamihi = Indeed; I cannot perceive; tam = as
such; anyatra = anyone other than garuDaat = Garuda (chief of the feathered race and
vehicle of Lord Vishnu); vaayoH = wind-god; anyatracha = and anyone other than;
hanuumataH = Hanuman; yaH = who; tareta = can cross; mahodadhim
= the great sea."
verses.translation:
"Indeed, I cannot perceive as such anyone other than Garuda (chief of the feathered
race and vehicle of Lord Vishnu), wind- god and Hanuman, who can cross the mighty ocean."

verses.sanskrit:
देवदानवयक्षाणाम् गन्धर्वोरगरक्षसाम् |

अप्रधृष्याम् पुरीम् लङ्काम् रावणेन सुरक्षिताम् || ६-१-४

प्रविष्टः सत्त्वमाश्रित्य जीवन्को नाम निष्क्रमेत् |
verses.translation:
"Who can return alive, having entered (once) taking refuge only in self- command,
the city of Lanka, which is unconquerable by gods demons and Yakshas and even by Gandharvas, Nagas
and ogres and well under the custody of Ravana?"

verses.sanskrit:
को विशेत्सुदुराधर्षाम् राक्षसैश्च सुरक्षिताम् || ६-१-५

यो वीर्यबलसम्पन्नो न समः स्याद्धनूमतः |
verses.translation:
"Who is able to capture that citadel, by assault, that is exceedingly dangerous to
be attacked and which is powerfully guarded by ogres, but one whose courage and valour are equal to
Hanuman's?"

verses.sanskrit:
भृत्यकार्यम् हनुमता सुग्रीवस्य कृतम् महत् || ६-१-६

एवम् विधाय स्वबलम् सदृशम् विक्रमस्य च |
verses.translation:
"A great act of service has been done by Hanuman to Sugriva thus by exhibiting his
strength corresponding to his pace."

verses.sanskrit:
यो हि भृत्यो नियुक्तः सन् भर्त्रा कर्मणि दुष्करे || ६-१-७

कुर्यात्तदुनुरागेण तमहुः पुरुषोत्तमम् |
verses.translation:
"That servant to whom his master entrusts a difficult task and who performs it with
zeal is said to be a superior person."

verses.sanskrit:
यो नियुक्तः परम् कार्यम् न कुर्यान्नऋपतेः प्रियम् || ६-१-८

भृत्यो युक्तः समर्थश्च तमाहुर्मध्यम् नरम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
8. aahuH = (they) speak; tam = of him; madhyamam
naram = as a mediocre man; yaH = which; bhR^ityaH = though qualified;
samarthaHcha = and capable; na kuryaat = does not perform; param =
superior; kaaryam = work; priyam = liked; nR^ipate = by the king."
verses.translation:
"The one who is ready and capable but who yet does no more than his master extracts
from him is called a mediocre person."

verses.sanskrit:
नियुक्तो नृपतेः कार्यम् न कुर्याद्यः समाहितः || ६-१-९

भृत्यो युक्तः समर्थश्च तमाहुः पुरुष्धमम् |
verses.translation:
"The one who is well and able and yet does not carry out the instructions of his
master as directed is said to be the least of men ."

verses.sanskrit:
तन्नियोगे नियुक्तेन कृतम् हनूमता || ६-१-१०

न चात्मा लघताम् नीतः सुग्रीवश्चापि तोषितः |
verses.wordMeaning:
10. tat = "for that reason; kR^ityam = the work;
kR^itam = was done; hanuumataa = by Hanumanta; niyuktaam = when
entrusted; niyoge = with duty (of search for Sita). Laghutaam = diminution; na
niitaH = was not brought; aatmaa = to his own self; sugriivashchaapi = and
Sugriva too; toshhitaH = was delighted."
verses.translation:
"Through the discovery of Sita's retreat by this faithful messenger, Hanuman has
fulfilled the task entrusted to him unfalteringly to the satisfaction of Sugriva and hence there
was no diminution to his self."

verses.sanskrit:
अहम् च रघवंशश्च लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः || ६-१-११

वैदेह्या दर्शनेनाद्य धर्मतः परिरक्षिताः |
verses.translation:
"By finding out Sita, the Raghu dynasty as well as myself and the valiant Lakshmana
too, have been rightly saved today."

verses.sanskrit:
इदम् तु मम दीनस्य मनो भूयः प्रकर्षति || ६-१-१२

यदिहास्य प्रियाक्ष्यातुर्न कुर्मि सदृशम् प्रियम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
12. tu = "but; prakarshhati = it squeezes; mama =
my; manaH = conscience; bhuuyaH = further; diinasya = hopeless as I am;
idam = (to think) that; yat na kurmi = I can not do; priyam = a pleasant
act; sadR^isham = worthy; asyam = of Hanuman; priyaaakhyaatuH = who has
informed me these good tidings."
verses.translation:
"But it squeezes my conscience further, hopeless as I am, to think that I am not
able to do a pleasant act befittingly to the bearer of these good tidings."

verses.sanskrit:
एष सर्वस्वभूतस्तु परिष्वङ्गो हनुमतः || ६-१-१३

मया कालमिमम् प्राप्य दत्तस्तस्य महात्मनः |
verses.wordMeaning:
13. eshhaH = aalingaH = "this embrace; sarvasya bhuutaH =
which is all that I can call really my possession; praapya = obtained; imam
kaalam = in these present circumstances; dattaH = is offered; mayaa = by me;
tasya mahaatmanaH = to such high soled; hanuumataH = Hanuman."
verses.translation:
"Let me at least embrace this magnanimous Hanuman since in the present
circumstances, this is all that is easily obtained from me."

verses.sanskrit:
इत्युक्त्वा प्रीतिहृष्टाङ्गो रामस्तम् परिष्स्वजे || ६-१-१४

हनुमन्तम् कृतात्मानम् क्ऱ्६इतवाक्यमुपागतम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
14. uktvaa = uttering; iti = thus; raamaH = Rama;
priiti hR^ishhTaangaH = whose limbs were thrilled with joy; parishhasvaje =
embraced; tam hanuumantam = that Hanuman; kR^itaatmaanam = who had mastered his
mind; upaagatam = (and) who had come; kR^ita vaakyam = having carried out his
word.

verses.sanskrit:
ध्यात्वा पुनरुवाचेदम् वचनम् रघुसत्तमः || ६-१-१५

हरीनामीश्वरस्यापि सुग्रीवस्योपशृण्वतः |
verses.wordMeaning:
15. dhyaatvaa = Reflecting (for a while); raghusattamaH =
Rama (the great scion of Raghus); punaH = again; uvaacha = spoke; idam
vachanam = these words; sugriivasya = Sugriva; iishvarasya = the ruler;
hariiNaam = of monkeys; upashR^iNvataH = listening arttentively.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वथा सुकृतम् तावत्सीतायाः परिमार्गणम् || ६-१-१६

सागरम् तु समासाद्य पुनर्नष्टम् मनो मम |
verses.translation:
"The search for Sita has been performed so much well in all ways. But my mind gets
dejected once more, when I behold this vast ocean."

verses.sanskrit:
कथम् नाम समुद्रस्य दुष्पारस्य महाम्भसः || ६-१-१७

हरयो दक्षिणम् पारम् गमिष्यन्ति समागताह् |
verses.wordMeaning:
17. katham = "how; harayaH = (these) monkeys;
samaagataH = put together; gamishhyanti = can obtain; dakshhiNam = the
southern; paaram = bank; samudrasya = of the ocean; dushhpaarasya =
which is difficult to cross; mahaambhasaH = and which contains voluminous water?"

verses.sanskrit:
यद्यप्येष तु वृत्तान्तो वैदेह्या गदितो मम || ६-१-१८

समुद्रपारगमने हरीणाम् किमिहोत्तरम् |
verses.translation:
"Having received the tidings of Sita, what can now be done to take the monkeys to
the farther side the sea?"

verses.sanskrit:
इत्युक्त्वा शोकसम्भ्रान्तो रामह् शत्रुनिबर्हणः || ६-१-१९

हनूमन्तम् महाबाहुस्ततो ध्यानमुपागमत् |
verses.wordMeaning:
19. iti = thus; uktvaa = speaking; hanunmantam = to
Hanumanta; raamaH = Rama; shatrunibarhaNaH = the destroyer of enemies;
mahaabaahuH = and the mighty armed; shoka sambhraantaH = was perplexed with
anguish; upaagamat = (then) became; dhyaanam = thoughtful."

sargas.title: Sarga 2
sargas.introduction:
Sugreeva comforts Rama, who is tormented with grief, asking him to keep away from
apprehensive mind and to think of ways and means to cross the ocean.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् तु शोकपरिद्यूनम् रामम् दशरथात्मजम् |

उवाच वचनम् श्रीमान् सुग्रीवह् शोकनाशनम् || ६-२-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. shriimaan = the glorious; sugriivaH = Sugreeva;
uvaacha = spoke; shokanaashanam = (these) grief-removing; vachanam =
words; tu = then; tam raamam\ to that Rama; dasharathaatmajam = the son of
Dasaratha; shokaparidyuunam = felt miserable by grief.

verses.sanskrit:
किम् त्वया तप्यते वीर यथान्यः प्राकृतस्तथा |

मैवम् भूस्त्यज सतापम् कृतघ्न इव सौहृदम् || ६-२-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. viira = "Oh; hero! Kim = why; tapyate = is it
lamented; tvayaa = by you; tathaa = thus; yathaa = as; anyaH =
any other; prakR^itaH = ordinary man? maa bhuuH = do not be(sorrowful); evam =
like this; tyaja = abandon; santaapam = grief; sauhR^idam iva = as
abandoning friendship; kR^itaghnaH = by an ungrateful man.
verses.translation:
"Oh, hero! Why are you lamenting thus, as any other ordinary man? Do not be
sorrowful like this. Abandon your grief, as an ungrateful man abandons friendship."

verses.sanskrit:
सम्तापस्य च ते स्थानम् न हि पश्यामि राघव |

प्रवृत्तामुपलब्धायाम् ज्ञाते च निलये रिपोः || ६-२-३
verses.translation:
"As the information about Seetha has since been gathered, I do not indeed see any
scope for your grief Oh, Rama!"

verses.sanskrit:
मतिमान् शास्त्रवित्प्राज्ञः पण्डितश्चासि राघव |

त्यजेमाम् प्राकृताम् उद्धिं कृतात्मेवार्थदूषणीम् || ६-२-४
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! You are a wise person, a knower of sacred works, an intellectual and a
learned man. Give up these ordinary apprehensions like a man whose spirit is disciplined gives up
ideas which spoil the purpose.

verses.sanskrit:
संद्रम् लङ्घयित्वा तु महानक्रसमाकुलम् |

लङ्कामारोहयिष्यामो हनिष्यामश्च ते रिपुम् || ६-२-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. aarohayishhyaamaH = "we shall make our way up; laN^kaam =
to Lanka; laN^ghayitvaa = by crossing; samudram = the ocean; mahaa nakra
samaakulam = which is filled with large crocodiles; hanishhyaamashcha = we shall
destroy; te = your; ripum = enemy.
verses.translation:
"We shall make our way up to Lanka, by crossing the ocean filled with large
crocodiles. We shall destroy your enemy."

verses.sanskrit:
निरुत्साहस्य दीनस्य शोकपर्याकुलात्मनः |

सर्वार्था व्यवसीदन्ति व्यसनम् चाधिगच्छति || ६-२-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. sarvaarthaaH = "All actions; vyavasiindanti = get
dissipated; nirutsaahasya = by a non-enthusiastic person; diinasya = a depressed
individual; shoka paryaakulaatmanaH = and a person disturbed with grief;
adhigachchhati = (such a person) obtains; vyasanamaH = troubles too."
verses.translation:
"All action get dissipated by a person who is non-enthusiastic, depressed and
disturbed with grief. Such a person gets into troubles too."

verses.sanskrit:
इमे शूराः समर्थाश्च सर्वतो हरियूथपाः |

त्वत्प्रियार्थम् कृतोत्साहाः प्रवेष्टुमपि पावकम् || ६-२-७

एषाम् हर्षेण जानामि तर्कश्चापि दृढो मम |
verses.translation:
"These leaders of monkey squads are gallant and efficient in all ways. They are
enthusiastic even to enter a fire for your sake. I understand this from their joy and my reasoning
too is sound."

verses.sanskrit:
विक्रमेण समानेष्ये सीताम् हत्वा यथा रिपुम् || ६-२-८

रावनम् पापक्र्माणम् तथा त्वम् कर्तुमर्हसि |
verses.translation:
"You ought to act in every way that I may be able to get back Seetha, after
destroying by an attack the enemy Ravana of sinful deeds."

verses.sanskrit:
सेतुरत्र यथा बद्ध्येथा पश्येम ताम् पुरीम् || ६-२-९

तस्य राक्षसराजस्य तथा त्वम् कुरु राघव |
verses.translation:
"You take steps in such a way that a bridge is constructed across the sea and we
reach that city of the king of ogres."

verses.sanskrit:
दृष्ट्वा ताम् हि पुरीम् लङ्काम् त्रिकूटशिखरे स्थिताम् || ६-२-१०

हतम् च रावणम् उद्धे दर्शनादवधारय |
verses.wordMeaning:
10. avadhaaraya = "be certain; raavaNam = that Ravana;
hatam = is killed; darshanaat = as soon as he is seen; yuddhe = in
combat; dR^ishhTvaa = and on our beholding; taam = that; laN^kaam puriim
= city of Lanka; sthitaam = standing; trikuuTa shikhare = on the peak of Trikuta
mountain.
verses.translation:
"Be certain that Ravana is killed, once he is seen in a battle and on our seeing
that city of Lanka standing on a peak of the Trikuta Mountain."

verses.sanskrit:
अबद्ध्वा सागरे सेतुम् घोरे च वरुणालये || ६-२-११

लङ्का न मर्दितुम् शक्या सेन्द्रैरपि सुरासुरैः |
verses.translation:
"Without building a bridge across the sea, the dreadful abode of Varuna (the god of
water), Lanka cannot be defeated even by gods and demons including Indra."

verses.sanskrit:
सेतुर्बद्धः समुद्रे च यावल्लङ्कासमीपतः || ६-२-१२

सर्वम् तीर्णम् च मे सैन्यम् जितमित्युपधारय |

इमे हि समरे वीरा हरयः कामरूपिणः || ६-२-१३
verses.translation:
"Know that when the whole of my army crosses the sea as a bridge is built across it,
they will come out victorious, because these monkeys who are able to change their form at will are
indeed valiant in battle."

verses.sanskrit:
तदलम् विक्लबाम् बुद्धिम् राजन् सर्वार्थनाशनीम् |

पुरुषस्य हि लोकेऽस्मिन् शोकः शौर्यापकर्षणः || ६-२-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. tat = "therefore; alam = (be) enough; viklabaam
= of apprehensive; buddhim = min; sarvaarthaa naashaniim = which destroys all
purposes; shokaH = (because) grief; asmin loke = in the world; shaurya
apakarshhaNaH = diminishes the valor; purushhasya = of a man; raajam = Oh
king!"
verses.translation:
"Therefore, take away your apprehensive mind, which destroys any enterprise for
grief in this world diminishes the might of a man, Oh king!"

verses.sanskrit:
यत्तु कार्यम् मनुष्येण शौण्डीर्यमवलम्ब्यताम् |

तदलम्करणायैव कर्तुर्भवति सत्वरम् || ६-२-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. avalambyataam = "cling to; kaaryam tu = the practice of;
shauNDiiryam = boldness; yat = which (is to be shown); manushhyeNa = by
a man; tat = It; bhavati = will produce; alamkaraNaayaiva = competence
truly; kartuH to the doer; satvaram = quickly."

verses.sanskrit:
अस्मिन् काले महाप्राज्ञ् सत्त्वमातिष्ठ ते जसा |

शूराणाम् हि मनुष्याणाम् त्वद्विधानाम् महात्मनाम् || ६-२-१६

विनष्टेवा रनस्स्टे वाशोकः सर्वार्थनाशनः |
verses.translation:
"Prevail upon the strength with alertness at this moment, Oh highly intelligent
prince! Grief for something lost or destroyed consumes all resources of even the strong and
magnanimous men like you."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्त्वम् बुद्धिमताम् श्रेष्ठह् सर्वशास्त्रर्थकोविदः || ६-२-१७

मद्विधैः सचिवैः सार्धमरिम् जेतुम् समर्हसि |
verses.translation:
"You are the foremost among the intelligent and the knower of all sacred texts.
Hence, you ought to defeat the enemy, with allies like me."

verses.sanskrit:
न हि पश्याम्यहम् कम् चित्त्रिषु लोकेषु राघव || ६-२-१८

गृहीतधनुषो यस्ते तिष्ठे दभिमुखो रणे |
verses.wordMeaning:
18. aham = I; na pashyaamihi = do not indeed see;
kamchit = any one; yaH = who; tishhThet = stands; abhimukhe =
face to face; raNe = on a battle field; raNe = on a battle field; te =
against you; trishhu lokeshhu = in the three worlds; gR^ishiita dhanushhaH =
(when you have) seized a bow; raaghava = Oh; Rama!
verses.translation:
"I do not indeed see any one who can withstand you in a battle filed in the three
worlds, when you are armed with a bow, Oh Rama!"

verses.sanskrit:
वानरेषु समासक्तम् न ते कार्यम् विपत्स्यते || ६-२-१९

अचिराद्द्रक्ष्यसे सीताम् तीर्त्वा सागरमक्षयम् |
verses.translation:
"You work duly entrusted to the monkeys will not be spoiled. You shall behold Seetha
era imperishable sea."

verses.sanskrit:
तदलम् शोकमालम्ब्य क्रोधमालम्ब भूपते || ६-२-२०

निश्चेष्टाह् क्षत्रिया मन्दाः सर्वे चण्डस्य् बिभ्यति |
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lord of the earth! Desist from this melancholy. Yield to your legitimate
indignation. Unadventurous Kshatriyas (members of warrior-tribe) never win honor but all fear the
wrathful."

verses.sanskrit:
लङ्घानार्थम् च घोरस्य समुद्रस्य नदीपतेः || ६-२-२१

सहास्माभिरिहोओपेतह् सूक्ष्मबुद्धिर्विचारय |
verses.wordMeaning:
21. suukshma buddhiH = (you with) sharp intellect; iha =
now; asmaabhiH saha = along with us; upetaH = together; ichaaraya =
think of; laN^ghanaartham cha = design to cross; samudrasya = the sea;
nadiipate = the lord rivers; ghorasya = which is terrible.
verses.translation:
"You with your resourceful mind, along with us together, ponder over now a design to
cross the sea, the terrible Lord of rivers."

verses.sanskrit:
लङ्घिते तत्र तैः सैन्यैर्जितमित्येव निश्चिनु || ६-२-२२

सर्वम् तीर्णम् च मे सैन्यम् जितमित्यवधार्यताम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
22. nishchinu = be assured; jitamityeva = of having
conquered; tatra = (once) that sea; laN^ghite = has been crossed; taiH
sainyaiH = by that army; avadhaaryataam = It is to be understood; jitamiti =
of having won; sarvam = (if) all; me = my; sainyam = forces;
tiirNam = arrive beyond ( the sea).
verses.translation:
"Know victory to be certain, once the sea has been crossed by the army. Verily when
all my forces have passed over the sea our triumph is assured!"

verses.sanskrit:
इमे हि हरयः शूराः समरे कामरूपिणः || ६-२-२३

तानरीन्विधमिष्यन्ति शिलापादपवृष्टिभिः |
verses.wordMeaning:
23. ime harayaH = These monkeys; shuuraaH = strong ones (as
they are); samare = in battle; kaama ruupeNa = and capable of assuming any form
at will; vidhamishhyantihi = will indeed blow away; taan = those; ariin
= enemies; shilaapaadapa vR^ishhTibhiH = by a shower of rocks and trees.
verses.translation:
"These monkeys, the courageous soldiers who are able to change their form at their
volition, will crush their opponents with an avalanche of rocks and trees."

verses.sanskrit:
कथम् चित्परिपश्यामि लङ्घितम् वरुणालयम् || ६-२-२४

हतमित्येव तम् मन्ये युद्धे शत्रुनिबर्हण |
verses.translation:
"Once we have crossed the sea (the abode of Varuna) by whatever the means employed,
Ravana is as definitely killed in my eyes, Oh exterminator of enemies!"

verses.sanskrit:
किमुक्त्वा बहुधा चापि सर्वथा विजया भवान् || ६-२-२५

निमित्तानि च पश्यामि मनो मे सम्प्रहृष्यति |

sargas.title: Sarga 3
sargas.introduction:
On hearing the words of Sugreeva, Rama requests Hanuma to describe Lanka in detail.
Hanuma gives a detailed description of Lanka as he saw it.

verses.sanskrit:
सुग्रीवस्य वचः श्रुत्वा हेतुमत् परम अर्थवित् |

प्रतिजग्राह काकुत्स्थो हनूमन्तम् अथ अब्रवीत् || ६-३-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. shrutvaa = hearing; hetumat = the well-founded;
paramaarthavat = and highly meaningful; vachaH = words; sugriivasya = of
Sugreeva; kaakutsthoH = Rama; pratijagraaha = concurred with him; atha =
(and) then; abraviit = spoke; hanuumantam = to Hanuman(as follows)

verses.sanskrit:
तरसा सेतु बन्धेन सागर उच्चोषणेन वा |

सर्वथा सुसमर्थो अस्मि सागरस्य अस्य लन्घने || ६-३-२
verses.translation:
"I am competent of crossing this ocean by all means, either by way of austerity or
by forming a bridge or by drying up the ocean."

verses.sanskrit:
कति दुर्गाणि दुर्गाया लंकायास् तद् ब्रवीहि मे |

ज्ञातुम् इच्चामि तत् सर्वम् दर्शनाद् इव वानर || ६-३-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. braviishhva = tell; me = me; durgaaNi =
citadels; laN^kaayaaH = in Lanka; durgaayaaH = are difficult of access;
vaanara = Oh Hanuman! ichchhaami = I desire; jJNaatum = to know; tat =
that; darshanaadiva = as though perceived with an eye.
verses.translation:
"Tell me how many citadels in Lanka are difficult of access, Oh, Hanuma! I desire to
know all that, as though viewed with an eye."

verses.sanskrit:
बलस्य परिमाणम् च द्वार दुर्ग क्रियाम् अपि |

गुप्ति कर्म च लंकाया रक्षसाम् सदनानि च || ६-३-४

यथा सुखम् यथावच् च लंकायाम् असि दृष्टवान् |

सरम् आचक्ष्व तत्त्वेन सर्वथा कुशलो हि असि || ६-३-५
verses.translation:
"You had the opportunity to catch a glimpse of the size of the army, the
fortification-details of the gates and citadels, the way in which Lanka was guarded and the various
mansions of ogres. Describe everything in accordance with facts, for you are skilled in all
ways."

verses.sanskrit:
श्रुत्वा रामस्य वचनम् हनूमान् मारुत आत्मजः |

वाक्यम् वाक्यविदाम् श्रेष्ठो रामम् पुनर् अथ अब्रवीत् || ६-३-६

श्रूयताम् सर्वम् आख्यास्ये दुर्ग कर्म विधानतः |

गुप्ता पुरी यथा लंका रक्षिता च यथा बलैः || ६-३-७
verses.translation:
"Listen to me! I shall tell you everything-how the city of Lanka has been defended
by the various methods of fortification and how it has been guarded by the troops."

verses.sanskrit:
राक्षसाश्च यथा स्निग्धा रावणस्य च तेजसा |

पराम् समृद्धिम् लंकायाः सागरस्य च भीमताम् || ६-३-८

विभागम् च बल ओघस्य निर्देशम् वाहनस्य च |

एवमुक्त्वा कपिश्रेष्ठः कथयामास तत्ववित् || ६-३-९
verses.translation:
"I shall tell you the details of how the ogres are attached to their king, the
excellent prosperity of Lanka generated by the glory of Ravana, the awfulness of the ocean, the
division of the body of his forces and of animals like horses and elephants carrying his forces
other than the infantry." Saying so, Hanuma the foremost among monkeys, who knew the truth narrated
as follows.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रहृष्टा मुदिता लंका मत्त द्विप समाकुला |

महती रथ सम्पूर्णा रक्षो गण समाकुला || ६-३-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. mahatii = the great; laN^kaa = Lanka; hR^ishhTa
pramuditaa = was rejoiced and gayful; matta dvipasamaakulaa = full of elephants in
rut; rathaa sampuurNaa = abounding in chariots; rakshhogaNa nishhevitaa = and
inhabited by gangs of ogres.
verses.translation:
"The great Lanka was rejoiced and gayful, full of elephants in rut, abounding in
chariots and inhabited by gangs of ogres."

verses.sanskrit:
दृढ बद्ध कवाटानि महापरिघवन्ति च |

चत्वारि विपुलान्यस्या द्वाराणि सुमहान्ति || ६-३-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. chatvaari = four; sumahaanti = fairly big;
vipulaani = and extensive; dvaaraaNi = gates; asyaaH = of the this
Lanka; dR^iDhabaddhakapaaTaani = are fitted with strong doors; mahaaparighavanti
= with huge beans for locking the gates.
verses.translation:
"Four fairly big and extensive gates are fitted with strong doors along with huge
beams for locking those gates."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्रेषूपयन्त्राणि बलवन्ति महान्ति च |

आगतम् पर सैन्यम् तैस् तत्र प्रतिनिवार्यते || ६-३-१२
verses.translation:
"Strong and mighty ballista capable of hurling darts and stones have been attached
to them. The enemy troops which arrive there are warded off by those catapults at the very
gates."

verses.sanskrit:
द्वारेषु संस्कृता भीमाः काल आयस मयाः शिताः |

शतशो रोचिता वीरैः शतघ्न्यो रक्षसाम् गणैः || ६-३-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. bhiimaaH = Dangerous; shitaaH = sharp edged;
shataghnyaH = Sataghnis; kaalaayasa mayaaH = made of iron; rachitaaH =
forged; gaNaiH = by hordes; rakshasaam = of ogres; samskR^itaH = have
been kept in readiness; shatashaH = in hundreds; dvaareshhu = at the gates.
verses.translation:
"Dangerous and sharp edged Sataghnis made of iron, forged by hordes of ogres, have
been kept in readiness in hundreds at the gates.
verses.commentary:
Comment: Sataghnis are four forearms in length bristled with iron spikes and are
so called because they are supposed to kill hundreds at a time.

verses.sanskrit:
सौवर्णः च महांस् तस्याः प्राकारो दुष्प्रधर्षणः |

मणि विद्रुम वैदूर्य मुक्ता विचरित अन्तरः || ६-३-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. mahaa praakaaraH = A great rampart; sauvarNaH = of gold;
tasyaaH = (encircle) that Lanka; dushhpadharshhaNaH = which is difficult to
assail with violence; maNividrumavaiduuryamuktaavirachitaantaraH = and is inlaid at
intervals with gems; corals; cat's eyes and pearls.
verses.translation:
"A great rampart made of gold, which is difficult to assail with violence and is
inlaid at intervals with gems, corals, cat's eyes and pearls, encircle that Lanka."

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वतः च महाभीमाः शीत तोया महाशुभाः |

अगाधा ग्राहवत्यः च परिखा मीन सेविताः || ६-३-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. mahaabhiimaaH = most fearful; agaadhaaH = and
fathomless; parikhaaH = deep trenches; mahaa shubhaiH = of great splendor;
shiitatoyaaH = filled with cold water; graahavatyashcha = infested with
alligators; mina sevitaaH = and inhabited by fishes; sarvataH = (are there) all
round (the city).
verses.translation:
"Most awe-inspiring and fathomless moats (deep trenches) of great splendor, filled
with cold water, infested with alligators and inhabited by fishes are there all round the
city."

verses.sanskrit:
द्वारेषु तासाम् चत्वारः सम्क्रमाः परम आयताः |

यन्त्रैर् उपेता बहुभिर् महद्भिर् दृढ संधिभिः || ६-३-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. dvaareshhu = in front of the gateways; chatvaaraH =
(there are) four; paramaayataaH = very extensive; taasaam samkramaaH =
draw-bridges; bahubhiH = equipped with numerous; yantraiH = engines;
upetaaH = and furnished with; mahadbhiH = considerable; buildings (built on the
rampart and meant for those entrusted with the duty of guarding the entrances).
verses.translation:
"Anterior to the gateways, there are four very extensive draw-bridges, equipped with
numerous engines and furnished with many rows of buildings (built on the rampart and meant for
those entrusted with the duty of guarding the entrances)."

verses.sanskrit:
त्रायन्ते सम्क्रमास् तत्र पर सैन्य आगमे सति |

यन्त्रैस् तैर् अवकीर्यन्ते परिखासु समन्ततः || ६-३-१७
verses.translation:
"Against the approach of the enemy forces there, the draw-bridges are protected by
the aforesaid engines and the enemy-battalions are flung into the moats on every side."

verses.sanskrit:
एकस् त्व् अकम्प्यो बलवान् सम्क्रमः सुमहादृढः |

काञ्चनैर् बहुभिः स्तम्भैर् वेदिकाभिः च शोभितः || ६-३-१८
verses.translation:
"The most important draw-bridge which is unshakable, firmly fastened very well and
strong, is dazzling with numerous gold pillars and pedestals."

verses.sanskrit:
स्वयम् प्रकृति सम्पन्नो युयुत्सू राम रावणः |

उत्थितः च अप्रमत्तः च बलानाम् अनुदर्शने || ६-३-१९
verses.translation:
"Ravana, inclined to war and endowed with powers of the state, himself remains alert
and in readiness to review the forces, Oh, Rama!"
verses.commentary:
Seven powers of the state are usually enumerated, viz. king, minister, allies,
treasure, army, territory and fortresses.

verses.sanskrit:
लंका पुरी निरालम्बा देव दुर्गा भय आवहा |

न अदेयम् पार्वतम् वन्यम् कृत्रिमम् च चतुर् विधम् || ६-३-२०
verses.translation:
"Lanka therefore is impregnable, it is a celestial citadel, that inspires terror.
Surrounded by water and built on a mountain, it has four fold defenses including forest and
artificial fortification."
verses.commentary:
Comment: Fourfold defences, defence by water, mountain, forest or by artificial
means.

verses.sanskrit:
स्थिता पारे समुद्रस्य दूर पारस्य राघव |

नौ पथः च अपि न अस्ति अत्र निरादेशः च सर्वतः || ६-३-२१
verses.translation:
"Lanka is situated on the other side of the ocean, which is difficult of access, Oh
Rama! It offers no passage for vehicles either and there is no proper communication from all
sides."

verses.sanskrit:
शैल अग्रे रचिता दुर्गा सा पूर् देव पुर उपमा |

वाजि वारण सम्पूर्णा लंका परम दुर्जया || ६-३-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. saa = that; puuH = city (of Lanka); deva
puropamaa = resembling the City of Gods; rachitaa = is built; shailaagre =
on a mountain peak; durgaaH = (and is inaccessible; saa = that; laN^kaa
= Lanka; vaajivaaraNa sampuurNaa = is abound with horses and elephants;
paramadurjayaa = and is extremely difficult to conquer.
verses.translation:
"That city of Lanka, resembling the city of Gods, is built on a mountain peak and is
inaccessible. That Lanka is abound with horses and elephants and is extremely difficult to
conquer."

verses.sanskrit:
परिघाः च शतघ्न्यः च यन्त्राणि विविधानि च |

शोभयन्ति पुरीम् लंकाम् रावणस्य दुरात्मनः || ६-३-२३

अयुतम् रक्षसाम् अत्र पश्चिम द्वारम् आश्रितम् |

शूल हस्ता दुराधर्षाः सर्वे खड्ग अग्र योधिनः || ६-३-२४
verses.translation:
"Ten thousand ogres all carrying darts in their hands and warriors contending with
swords, who are difficult to assail, are positioned at the eastern gate."

verses.sanskrit:
नियुतम् रक्षसाम् अत्र दक्षिण द्वारम् आश्रितम् |

चतुर् अन्गेण सैन्येन योधास् तत्र अपि अनुत्तमाः || ६-३-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. niyutam = one hundred thousand; rakshasaam = of ogres;
aashritam = are positioned; atra = there; dakshhiNa dvaaram = at the
southern gate; tatraapi = there also; yodhaaH = warriors; anuttamaaH =
unsurpassed by others; sainyena = constitute the army; chaturaN^geNa = with its
four limbs(viz. horses; foot soldiers; elephants and chariots).
verses.translation:
"One hundred thousand of ogres, with an army of four limbs (viz. horses, foot
soldiers, elephants and chariots) are positioned at the southern gate of the city. Warriors
unsurpassed by others constitute that army."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रयुतम् रक्षसाम् अत्र पूर्व द्वारम् समाश्रितम् |

चर्म खड्ग धराः सर्वे तथा सर्व अस्त्र कोविदाः || ६-३-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. prayutam = one million; rakshasaam = ogres;
atra = there; aashritam = are positioned; pashchima dvaaram = at the
western gate; sarve = all of them; charmakhaDgadharaaH = carry shields and
swords; tathaa = and; sarvaastrakovidaH = proficient in the use of all mystic
missiles.

verses.sanskrit:
न्यर्बुदम् रक्षसाम् अत्र उत्तर द्वारम् आश्रितम् |

रथिनः च अश्व वाहाः च कुल पुत्राः सुपूजिताः || ६-३-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. nyarbudam = a hundred millions; rakshasaam = of ogres;
atra = there; aashritam = are positioned; uttaradvaaram = at the
northern gate; rathinaH = (they are) car-warriors; ashvavaahaashcha =
horse-soldiers; kulaputraaH = sons of noble families; supuujitaaH = and greatly
honored."
verses.translation:
"A hundred millions of ogres, who are mounted in chariots or who ride on hoses, sons
of distinguished families and greatly honored, are positioned there at the northern gate."

verses.sanskrit:
शतम् शत सहस्राणाम् मध्यमम् गुल्मम् आश्रितम् |

यातु धाना दुराधर्षाः साग्र कोटिः च रक्षसाम् || ६-३-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. atha = moreover; yaatudhaanaaH = ogres; shatashaH
sahasraaNi = hundreds of thousands in number; duraadarshhaaH = difficult to assail;
saagrakoTishcha = aggregating to one and quarter of a crore; rakshasaam = of
ogres; aashritaaH = are positioned; madhyamaskandham = at the central
division."
verses.translation:
"Moreover, ogres hundreds of thousands in number, difficult to attack, aggregating
to one and quarter of a crore of ogres are positioned at the central division."

verses.sanskrit:
ते मया सम्क्रमा भग्नाः परिखाः च अवपूरिताः |

दग्धा च नगरी लंका प्राकाराः च अवसादिताः || ६-३-२९

बलैकदेशः क्षपितो राक्षसानाम् महात्मनाम् |
verses.translation:
"Those bridges were broken by me and the moats were filled up (with the wreckage).
The city of Lanka was burnt by me and the defensive walls were pulled down. A part of the army of
the gigantic ogres was destroyed."

verses.sanskrit:
येन केन तु मार्गेण तराम वरुण आलयम् || ६-३-३०

हता इति नगरी लंकाम् वानरैर् अवधार्यताम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
30. yena kena tu maargeNa = by some way or other; taraama =
we shall cross; varuNaalayam = the ocean; laN^kaanagarii = the city of Lanka;
upadhaaryataam = should be assumed; hataa iti = as destroyed; vaanaraiH
= by the monkeys."By some way or other; let us cross the ocean. The city of Lanka should be assumed
then as destroyed by the monkeys."
verses.translation:
"By some way or other, let us cross the ocean. The city of Lanka should be assumed
then as destroyed by the monkeys."

verses.sanskrit:
अङ्गदो द्विविदो मैन्दो जाम्बवान् पनसो नलः || ६-३-३१

नीलः सेना पतिः चैव बल शेषेण किम् तव |

प्लवमाना हि गत्वा ताम् रावणस्य महापुरीम् || ६-३-३२

सप्रकाराम् सभवनाम् आनयिष्यन्ति मैथिलीम् |

सप्राकाराम् सभवनामानयुष्यन्ति राघव || ६-३-३३
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! What is the use for the rest of the forces to you? Angada, Divida,
Mainda, Jambavan, Panasa, Anala and Nila the commander-in-chief alone, by reaching that great city
of Ravana leaping and storming Lanka with its mountains and woods, moats and archways, protective
walls and buildings, will recover Seetha."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् आज्ञापय क्षिप्रम् बलानाम् सर्व सम्ग्रहम् |

मुहूर्तेन तु युक्तेन प्रस्थानमभिरोचय || ६-३-३४
verses.wordMeaning:
34. aaJNaapaya = order; kshhipram = quickly; balaanaam
sarvasangraham = to get all the forces; evam = accordingly; abhirochaya = be
inclined; prasthaanam = for the march; yuktena = at the appropriate moment.
verses.translation:
"Order quickly to get all the forces accumulated accordingly. Let us set out at the
appropriate moment."
verses.commentary:
