sargas.title: Sarga 31
sargas.introduction:
Agitated to learn that Rama had arrived to Lanka and is stationed at Suvela 
			mountain along with his army, Ravana confers with his ministers and after dismissing them, 
			approaches Seetha with Vidyujjuha, knower of conjuring tricks. Informing Seetha that her 
			husband along with his army had been killed in battle, he produces before her an illusive 
			Rama's head along with bow and arrows created with Vidyujjuha's conjuring trick. Thus, Ravana 
			deceives Seetha about the death of Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
ततस्तमक्षोभ्यबलम् लङ्कायां नृपतेश्चराः |

			सुवेले राघवं शैले निविष्टं प्रत्यवेदयन् || ६-३१-१

चाराणाम् रावणः श्रुत्वा प्राप्तम् रामम् महाबलम् |

			जातोद्वेगोऽभवत्किंचित्सचिवानिदमब्रवीत् || ६-३१-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. shrutvaa = Hearing; chaaraaNaam = throught; spies;
			raamam = that Rama; mahaabalam = with his mighty army; praaptam 
			= had arrived; raavaNaH = Ravana; jaatodvegaH = was agitated; kimchit 
			= a little; abraviit = and spoke; idam = thse words; sachivaan 
			= to his counsellors (as follows):

verses.sanskrit:
मन्त्रिणः शीघ्रमायान्तु सर्वे वै सु समाहिताः |

			अयम् नो मन्त्रकालो हि सम्प्रास्त इति राक्षसाः || ६-३१-३
verses.translation:
"The time for deliberation has come to us. Let our ministerial colleagues 
			come quickly with their keenly attentive minds."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्य तच्चासनम् श्रुत्वा मन्त्रिणोऽभ्यागमन् द्रुतम् |

			ततः स मन्त्रयामास राक्षसैः सचिवैः सह || ६-३१-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. shrutvaa = hearing; tat = that; shaasanam 
			= command; tasya = of his; mantriNaH = the ministers; abhyaagamam 
			= came; drutam = soon; tataH = thereafter; saH = he; mantrayaamaasa 
			= deliberated; sachivaiH saha = with his ministers; raakshhasaiH = the 
			ogres.

verses.sanskrit:
मन्त्रयित्वा तु दुर्धर्षः क्षमं यत्तदन्न्तरम् |

			विसर्जयित्वा सचिवान् प्रविवेश स्वमालयम् || ६-३१-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. durdharshhaH = Ravana; the dreadful one; mantrayitvaa 
			= deliberated; tat = that; yat = which; kshhamam = was befitting 
			to be done; anantaram = immediately after; visarjayitvaa = sent away sachivaan 
			= the ministers; pravivesha = and entered; svam his; aalayam = palace.

verses.sanskrit:
ततो राक्षसमादाय विद्युज्जिह्वम् महाबलम् |

			मायाविदम् महामायः प्रविशद्यत्र मैथिली || ६-३१-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. tataH = thereafter; mahaamayaH = Ravana; practising 
			conjuring tricks; aadaaya = took; vidyujjihvam = Vidyujjihva; mahaabalam 
			= exceedingly strong; maayaavidam = who knew magic spells (along with him); 
			praavishat = entered; yatra = the place where; maithilii = Seetha 
			( was there).

verses.sanskrit:
विद्युज्जिह्वम् च मायाज्ञ्मब्रवीद्राक्षसाधिपः |

			मोहयिष्यावहे सीताम् मायया जनकात्मजाम् || ६-३१-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. raakshaasaadhipaH = Ravana the lord of demons; abraviit 
			cha = spoke; vidyujjihvam = to Vidyujjihva; maagaajN^gam = who knew 
			deceptions (as follows): mohayishhvaavahe maayayaa = we shall create an illusion to deceive;
			siitaam = Seetha; janakaatmajaam = the daughter of Janaka.

verses.sanskrit:
शिरो मायामयम् गृह्य राघवस्य विशाचर |

			मां त्वं समुपतिष्ठस्व महच्च सशरम् धनुः || ६-३१-८
verses.translation:
"O, demon! Produce a head resembling Rama's along with a great bow and arrows 
			created deceptively and present yourself before me."

verses.sanskrit:
एवमुक्त स्तथेत्याह विद्युज्जिह्वो निशाचतः |

			दर्शयामास ताम् मायाम् सुप्रयुक्ताम् स रावणे || ६-३१-९

			तस्य तुष्टोऽभवद्राजा प्रददौ च विभूषणम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
9. evam = thus; uktaH = spoken; nishaacharaH 
			= the demon; vidyujjihvaH = Vidyujjihva; aaha = said; tatheti 
			= "be itso"; saH = he; darshayaamaasa = showed; raavaNe = to 
			Ravana; taam maayaam = that conjuring trick; suprayuktaam = well-performed;
			raajaa = the king; abravat = became well-performed; raajaa = 
			the king; abhaat = became; tushhTaH = satisfied; tasya = of him;
			pradadecha = and gave too; vibhuushhaNam = an ornament.

verses.sanskrit:
अशोकवनिकायाम् च सीतादर्शनलालसः || ६-३१-१०

			नैरृतानामधिपतिः सम्विवेश महाबलः |
verses.wordMeaning:
10. mahaabalaH = the exceedingly strong; nairR^itaanaam 
			adhipatiH = Ravana the suzerain lord of demons; samvivesha = duly entered;
			ashoka vanikaayaam = the Ashoka grove; siitaadarshana laalasaH = eagerly 
			longng for the sight of Seetha.

verses.sanskrit:
ततो दीनामदैन्यार्हाम् ददर्श धनदामजः || ६-३१-११

			अधोमुखीं शोकपरामुपविष्टाम् महीतले |

			भर्तारमेव ध्यायन्तीमशोकवनिकाम् गताम् || ६-३१-१२

उपास्यमानाम् घोराभी राक्षसीभिरदूरतः |

			उपसृत्य ततः सीताम् प्रहर्षं नाम कीर्तयन् || ६-३१-१३

			इदम् च वचनम् धृष्टमुवाच जनकात्मजाम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
13. tataH = then; upasR^itya = approaching; siitaam 
			= Seetha; upaasyamaanaam = who was attended upon; ghoraabhiH raakshhasiibhiH 
			= by ghastly ogresses; aduurataH = not for distant from her; kiirtayan praharshham 
			naama = and mentioning his own name with great delight; uvaacha = spoke;
			idam vachanam = these words; dhR^ishhTam = whih were audacious; janakaatmajam 
			= to Seetha.

verses.sanskrit:
सान्त्व्यमाना मया भद्रे यमाश्रित्य विमन्यसे || ६-३१-१४

			खरहन्ता स ते भर्ता राघवः समरे हतः |
verses.translation:
"O, Beautiful one! He in whom you trusted and repelled me when I sought to 
			console you - that murderer of Khara, your husband Rama has been killed in battle."

verses.sanskrit:
चिन्नम् ते सर्वथा मूलम् दर्पश्च विहतो मया || ६-३१-१५

			व्यसनेनात्मनः सीते मम भार्या भविष्यसि |
verses.wordMeaning:
15. siite = O Seetha!; te muulam = your root; 
			chhinnam = has been cut off; sarvathaa = in all ways; mayaa = be 
			me; darpashha = bhavishhyasi = you will become; mama = my; bhaaryaa 
			= wife; aatmaanaH = by your; vyasanana = evil predicament.
verses.translation:
"O, Seetha! Your root has been cut off in all ways by me and your pride has 
			been slain. By your present evil predicament, you will become my wife."

verses.sanskrit:
विसृजैतां मतिं मूढे किं मृतेन करिष्यसि || ६-३१-१६

			भवस्व भद्रे भार्याणां सर्वासामीश्वरी मम |
verses.wordMeaning:
16. muuDhe = O; Foolish one!; visR^ija = give up 
			etaam = this; matim = resolution; kim kariSyasi = what will you do;
			mR^itena = with one dead?; bhadre = O; beautiful one!; Bhave 
			= become; iishvarii = the Chief Queen; sarvaasaam = over all; mama 
			= my; bharyaaNaam = consorts.
verses.translation:
"O, Foolish one! Give up this resolution. What will you do with one dead. 
			O, beautiful one! Become the Chief Queen over all my consorts."

verses.sanskrit:
अल्पपुण्ये निवृत्तार्थे मूढे पण्डितमानिनि || ६-३१-१७

			सृणु भर्तृनधम् सीते घोरं वृत्रवधं यथा |
verses.wordMeaning:
17. munDhe = O; foolish one; alpapuNye = with scant 
			virtue; nivR^ittaarthe = and lost purpose!; panN^Dita maanini = you think 
			yourself as wise! Siite = O; Seetha!; shR^iNu = hear; ghoram bhartR^ivadham 
			= about the dreadful killing of your husband; vR^itra vadham yathaa = as the killing 
			of the demon vR^itra(at the hands of Indra).
verses.translation:
"O, Foolish one, with scant virtue and failed purpose! You think yourself 
			as wise! O, Seetha! Hear about the dreadful killing of your husband in battle, as the killing 
			of the demon Vritra (at the hands of Indra)"

verses.sanskrit:
समायातः समुद्रान्तं हन्तुं मां किल राघवः || ६-३१-१८

			वानरेन्द्रप्रणीतेन बलेव महता वृतः |
verses.wordMeaning:
18. raaghavaH = Rama; vR^itaH = encircled; mahataa 
			balena = by an extensive army; vaanarendra praNiitena = led forwards by sugreeva;
			samaayaataH kila = at the sea- shore; hantum = to kill; maam 
			= me.
verses.translation:
"Rama, encircled by an extensive army led forward by Sugreeva, is said to 
			have arrived at the sea-shore, to kill me."

verses.sanskrit:
सम्निविष्टः समुद्रस्य पीड्य तीरमथोत्तरम् || ६-३१-१९

			बलेन महता रामो व्रजत्यस्तम् दिवाकरे |
verses.wordMeaning:
19. atha = thereafter; (while); divaakare = the sun;
			vrajati = was going; astam = to set; raamaH = Rama; savishhTaH 
			= encamped; piiDya = encroaching upon; uttaram = the northern; tiiram 
			= shore; samudrasya = of sea; mahataa balena = with his mighty army.
verses.translation:
"While sun was going to set, Rama with his mighty army was encamped there, 
			encroaching upon the northern shore of the sea."

verses.sanskrit:
अथाध्वनि परिश्रान्तमर्धरात्रे स्थितम् बलम् || ६-३१-२०

			सुखसुप्तं समासाद्य चरितम् प्रथमं चरैः |
verses.wordMeaning:
20. atha = then; balam = their army; sthitam 
			= stationed there; sukhasuptam = omfortably asleep; ardharaatre = at the 
			middle of the night; parishraantam = and completely fatigued; adhvani 
			= in the course of the journey; samaasaadya = was approached; prathamam 
			= first; charitam = and spied; charaiH = by our spies.
verses.translation:
"Their army stationed there, comfortably asleep at the middle of the night 
			and completely fatigued in the course of the journey, was approached first and espied by 
			our spies."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्प्रहस्तप्रणीतेन बलेन महता मम || ६-३१-२१

			बलमस्य हतम् रात्रौ यत्र रामः सलक्ष्मणः |
verses.translation:
"By my great army, which was led by Prahasta, Rama's army was killed in the 
			night at the place where Rama and Lakshmana were there."

verses.sanskrit:
पट्टिशान् परिघांश्चक्रानृष्टीर्दण्डान्महायुधान् || ६-३१-२२

			बाणजालानि शूलानि भास्वरान् कूटमुद्गरान् |

			यष्टीश्च तोमरान् प्रासांश्चक्राणि मुसलानि च || ६-३१-२३

			उद्यम्योद्यम्य रक्षोभिर्वानरेषु निपातिताः |

अथ सुप्तस्य रामस्य प्रहस्तेन प्रमाथिना || ६-३१-२४

			असक्तम् कृतहस्तेन शिरश्छिन्नं महासिना |
verses.translation:
"The impetuous Prahasta, with his steady hand, severed the head of Rama sleeping 
			there, with his mighty sword without a resistance from any one."

verses.sanskrit:
विभीषणः समुत्पत्य निगृहीतो यदृच्छया || ६-३१-२५

			दिशम् प्रव्राजितः सैन्यैर्लक्ष्मणः प्लवगैः सह |
verses.wordMeaning:
25. yard^ichchhayaa = fortunately; vibhiiSaNaH = 
			Vibhishana; samut[atua = was overleaped; nigR^ihiitaH = and hel aptive;
			lakshhmaNaH = Lakshmana; plavangaiH saha = along with monkeys; pravraajitaH 
			= were made to run away; disham = in all diretions.
verses.translation:
"Fortunately, Vibhishana was over leaped and held captive. Lakshmana along 
			with the monkeys were made to run away in all directions."

verses.sanskrit:
सुग्रीवो ग्रीवया सीते भग्नया प्लवगाधिपः || ६-३१-२६

			निरस्तहनुकः श्रेते हनुमान् राक्षसैःर्हतः |
verses.translation:
"O, Seetha! Sugreeva the king of monkeys with his neck broken and Hanuman 
			with his jaw removed were laid down and killed by the demons."

verses.sanskrit:
जाम्बवानथ जानुभ्यामुत्पतन्निहतो युधि || ६-३१-२७

			पट्टिशैर्बहुभिश्छन्नो विकृत्तः सादपो यथा |
verses.wordMeaning:
27. atha = thereafter; jaambavaan = Jambavan; 
			utpatan = who was rising up; jaanubhyaam = on his knees; nihataH 
			= was killed; yudhi = in the combat; chhinnaH = (He was) cut off; bahubhiH 
			with many; paTTishaiH = sharp-edged spears; paadapoyatha = like a tree.
verses.translation:
"Thereafter Jambavan who was rising up on his knees was killed in the battle. 
			His body was cut off with many sharp-edged spears, like a tree is cut off."

verses.sanskrit:
मैन्दश्च द्विविदश्चोभौ तौ वानरवरर्षभौ || ६-३१-२८

			निःश्वसन्तौ रुदन्तौ च रुधिरेण परीवृतौ |

			असिना व्यायतौ चिन्नौ मध्ये ह्यरिनिषूदनौ || ६-३१-२९
verses.translation:
"Mainda and Dvivida - those two great scourgers of their foes, the long bodied 
			and the foremost of the monkeys, groaning and breathless, their limbs bathed in blood-were 
			cut to pieces at the waist-level, by a sword."

verses.sanskrit:
अनुष्वनति मेदिन्याम् पनसः यथा || ६-३१-३०

			वाराचैर्बहुभिश्छन्नः श्रेते दर्याम् दरीमुखः |

			कुमुदस्तु महातेजा निष्कूजन् सायकैर्हतः || ६-३१-३१
verses.translation:
"Panasa, crying for help was stretched on the earth under a tree of the same 
			name. Pierced by lying in a pit. The exceedingly valiant kumuda on his part, shrieking, 
			was killed by a hail of missiles."

verses.sanskrit:
अङ्गदो बहुभिश्छ्न्नः शरैरासाद्य राक्षसैः |

			परितो रुधिरोद्गारी क्षितौ निपतिताङ्गदः || ६-३१-३२
verses.translation:
"Angada, who was adorned with bracelets lies fallen down, emitting blood 
			on all sides, as he was pierced by the demons with many arrows, after approaching him."

verses.sanskrit:
हरयो मथिता वागैरथ जालैस्तथापरे |

			शयाना मृदितास्तत्र वायुवेगैरिवाम्बुदाः || ६-३१-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. apare = other; harayaH = monkeys; shayanaaH 
			= sleeping; tatra = there; mathitaa = were crushed; naagai = 
			by elephants; tathaa = and; rathajaalaiH = multitude of chariots; 
			ambudaaH iva = like clouds; m^iditaa = are crushed; vaayuvegaiH = 
			by the velocity of wind.
verses.translation:
"The monkeys sleeping there were crushed by elephants and multitude of chariots, 
			like clouds are crushed to pieces by the velocity of wind."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रसृताश्च परे त्रस्ताः हन्यमाना जघन्यतः |

			अनुद्रुतास्तु रक्षोBहिः सिम्हैरिव महाद्विपाः || ६-३१-३४
verses.wordMeaning:
34. pare = the enemy; prasR^itaaH = fled; trastaaH 
			= in terror; anudrutaaH; pursued; rakshhobhiH = by demons; hanyamaanaaH 
			= while being struck; jaghanyataH = on the back; simhaiH iva = like lions;
			mahaadvipaaH = pursuing great elephants.
verses.translation:
"The enemy fled in terror, pursued by demons, while being struck on the bak, 
			like lions pursuing great elephants."

verses.sanskrit:
सागरे पतिताः केचित्केचिद्गगनमाश्रिताः |

			ऋक्षा वृक्षामपारूढा वानरैर्व्यतिमिश्रिताः || ६-३१-३५
verses.wordMeaning:
35. kechit = some; patitaaH = fell; saagare 
			= in the ocean; kechit = some; aashritaaH = sought shelter; gaganam 
			= in the sky; R^ikshhaaH = the bears; vyatinishritaaH = combined; 
			vaanaraiH = with monkeys; upaaruuDhaaH = climbed; viR^ikshhaan = 
			the trees.
verses.translation:
"Some monkeys fell in the sea and some sought shelter in the sky. The bears 
			with the monkeys climbed the trees."

verses.sanskrit:
सागरस्य च तीरेषु शैलेषु च वनेषु च |

			पिङ्गलास्ते विरूपाक्षे राक्षसैर्बहवो हताः || ६-३१-३६
verses.wordMeaning:
36. raakshhasaiH = by the demons; viruupaakshhaiH 
			= with monstrous eyes; hataaH = were killed; te = those; bahavaH 
			= multitude; piN^galaaH = of monkeys; tiireshhu = at the shores; saagarasya 
			= of the sea; shaileshhuha = in the mountains vaneshhuha = and in the woods.
verses.translation:
"By the demons with monstrous eyes, were killed a multitude of monkeys, at 
			the shores of the sea, in the mountains an in the woods."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् तव हतो भर्ता ससैन्यो मम सेवया |

			क्षतजार्द्रं रजोध्वस्तमिदं चाप्याहृतम् शिरः || ६-३१-३७
verses.translation:
"Thus, your husband together with his army was killed by my troops. This 
			head, drenched in his blood and besmeared with dust has been gathered up."

verses.sanskrit:
ततः परमदुर्धर्षो रावणो राक्षसेश्वरः |

			सीतायामुपशृण्वत्यां राक्षसीमिद मब्रवीत् || ६-३१-३८

राक्षसम् क्रूरकर्माणम् विद्युज्जिह्वम् समानय |

			येन तद्रघनशिरः सङ्ग्रमात्स्वयमाहृतम् || ६-३१-३९
verses.wordMeaning:
39. samaanaya = bring; vidyujjihvam = Vidyujjihva;
			raakshhasam = the demons; kruurakarmaaNam = of the cruel deed; yena 
			= by whom; tat = that; raaghava shiraH = Raghava's head; aahR^itam 
			= was brought; svayam = by himself; saN^graamaat = from the battle-field.
verses.translation:
"Bring Vidyujjihva, the demon of the cruel deed, by whom himself Raghava's 
			head was brought back from the battle-field."

verses.sanskrit:
विद्युज्जिह्व स्तदा गृह्य शिरस्तत्सशरासनम् |

			प्रणामम् शिरसा कृत्वा रावणस्याग्रतः स्थितः || ६-३१-४०

तमब्रवीत्ततो राजा रावणो राक्षसम् स्थितम् |

			विद्युज्जिह्वम् महाजिह्वम् समीपपरिवर्तिनम् || ६-३१-४१
verses.wordMeaning:
41. tataH = thereafter; raajaa = the king; raavaNaH 
			= Ravana; abraviit = spoke; tam vidyujjihvam = to that Vidyujjihva;
			raakshhasam = the demon; mahaajihvam = who had a huge tongue; sthitam 
			= and who was standing; samiipa parivartinam = and moving closely.

verses.sanskrit:
अग्रतः कुरु सीतायाः श्रीघ्रं दाशरथेः शिरः |

			अवस्थां पशिचमां भर्तुः कृपणा साधु पश्यतु || ६-३१-४२
verses.translation:
"Keep Rama's head immediately in front of Seetha. Let the miserable Seetha 
			see well the final condition of her husband.

verses.sanskrit:
एवमुक्तं तु तद्रक्षः शिरस्तत्प्रियदर्शनम् |

			उपनिक्षिप्य सीतायाः क्षिप्रमन्तरधीयत || ६-३१-४३
verses.wordMeaning:
43. tat rakshhaH = that demon; evam = thus; uktam 
			= spoken; upamikshhipya = desposited in the vicinity; siitaayaaH = of 
			Seetha; tat = that; shiraH = head; priyadarshanam = whih was 
			pleasant to look at; kshhipram = and soon; antaradhiiyata = disappeared 
			from the site.

verses.sanskrit:
रावणश्चापि चिक्षेप भास्वरम् कार्मुकम् महत् |

			त्रिषु लोकेषु विख्यातम् रामस्यैतदिति ब्रुवन् || ६-३१-४४
verses.wordMeaning:
44. etat = this is; raamasya = Rama's; vikhyaatam 
			= (bow) famous; tishhu lokeshhu = in the three worlds; raavaNashhaai = 
			Ravana; bruvan = speaking; iti = thus; hikshhepa = threw ( in 
			front of Seetha); bhaasvaram = the shining; mahat = great; kaarmukam 
			= bow.
verses.translation:
"Here is Rama's bow famous in the three worlds!" - saying thus, Ravana threw 
			a great shining bow in front of Seetha.

verses.sanskrit:
इदम् तत्तव रामस्य कार्मुकं ज्यासमाव्ऱ्६इतम् |

			इह प्रहस्तेवानीतम् तम् हत्वा निशि मानुषम् || ६-३१-४५
verses.wordMeaning:
45. idam = this is; tat = that; kaarmukam 
			= bow; jyaa samaavR^itam = with its cord stretched; tava raamasya = belonging 
			to Rama; aaniitam = that was brought back; prahastena = by Prahasta;
			iha = here; hatvaa = after killing; tam = that; maanushham 
			= man; nishi = in the night.
verses.translation:
"This is the bow with its cord stretched, belonging to Rama, that was brought 
			back by Prahasta here, after killing that hero in the night."

verses.sanskrit:
स विद्युजिह्वेन सहैव तच्छिरो |

			धमश्च भूमौ विनिकीर्य रावणः |

			विदेहराजस्य सुताम् यशस्विनीं |

			ततोऽब्रवीत्ताम् भव मे वशामुगा || ६-३१-४६
verses.wordMeaning:
46. saH aavaNaH = that Ravana; vidyujihvena sahaiva 
			= along with Vidyujjihva; vinikiirya = threw; tat = that; shiraH 
			= head; dhanushha = and the bow; bhuumau = on the ground; taam 
			= towards Seetha; yashasviniim = the illustrious; sutaam = daughter;
			videharaajasya = of Janaka; abraviit = and said; bhava = you 
			become; rashaamgaa = but missive; me = to me.

sargas.title: Sarga 32
sargas.introduction:
Seetha was shocked, fell unconscious at the sight of Rama's head and bow. She regained 
consciousness and asked Ravana to kill her also, so that she would join her husband in heaven. Meanwhile, 
Ravana is abruptly called away by an emissary and after consultation with his ministers, prepares his 
army for action against the forces of Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
सा सीता तच्चिरो दृष्ट्वा तच् च कार्मुकम् उत्तमम् | |

सुग्रीव प्रतिसंसर्गम् आख्यातम् च हनूमता || ६-३२-१

नयने मुख वर्णम् च भर्तुस् तत् सदृशम् मुखम् |

केशान् केश अन्त देशम् च तम् च चूडा मणिम् शुभम् || ६-३२-२

एतैह् सर्वैर् अभिज्नानैर् अभिज्नाय सुदुह्खिता |

विजगर्हे अथ कैकेयीम् क्रोशन्ती कुररी यथा || ६-३२-३

सकामा भव कैकेयि हतो अयम् कुल नन्दनः |

कुलम् उत्सादितम् सर्वम् त्वया कलह शीलया || ६-३२-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. kaikeyii = O;Kaikeyi ;bhava = = be; sakaamaa = an accomplisher 
of your desires; ayam = this Rama; kula nandanaH = causing joy to the family;hato 
= was killed; sarvam = the entire;kulam = race; utsaaditam = has been destroyed;
tvayaa = by you; kalaha shiilayaa = of quarrelsome nature.

verses.sanskrit:
आर्येण किम् नु कैकेय्याः कृतम् रामेण विप्रियम् |

यन्मया चीर वसनस् तया प्रस्थापितो वनम् || ६-३२-५
verses.translation:
"You gave clothes made of bark to the venerable Rama and sent him on exile to the forest 
along with me. What harm has he done to you?"

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्त्वा तु वैदेही वेपमाना तपस्विनी |

जगाम जगतीम् बाला चिन्ना तु कदली यथा || ६-३२-६

सा मुहूर्तात् समाश्वस्य प्रतिलभ्य च चेतनाम् |

तत् शिरह् समुपाघ्राय विललाप आयत ईक्षणा || ६-३२-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. saa = she; aayatekshhaNaa = having wide eyes; atha 
= thereafter; samaashvaasya = was consoled; muhuurtaat = in a moment; parilabhya 
= obtained; chetanaam = consciousness; samupaasthaaya = approached near; tat 
= that; shiraH = head; vilaapa = and lamented.

verses.sanskrit:
हा हता अस्मि महा बाहो वीर व्रतम् अनुव्रता |

इमाम् ते पश्चिम अवस्थाम् गता अस्मि विधवा कृता || ६-३२-८
verses.translation:
"O, the long armed Rama! The pursuer of a strong will! Alas, you have been killed! I 
have become a witness to your last fate. I have been made a widow."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रथमम् मरणम् नार्या भर्तुर् वैगुण्यम् उच्यते |

सुवृत्तः साधु वृत्तायाः सम्वृत्तस् त्वम् मम अग्रतः || ६-३२-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. uchyate = it is said; prathamam = the early; maraNam 
= death; bhartuH = of a husband; vaiguN^yam = is a faultiness; naaryaah = 
of the woman; tvam = you; suvR^itah = having good conduct; samvR^ittaH = 
died; agrataH = earlier; mama = to me; saadhu vR^ittaayaaH = well surviving.
verses.translation:
"It is said that the early death of a husband is the misfortune of a wife. You, having 
good conduct, have preceded me in death."

verses.sanskrit:
दुह्खाद् दुह्कःअम् प्रपन्नाया मग्नायाः शोक सागरे |

यो हि माम् उद्यतस् त्रातुम् सो अपि त्वम् विनिपातितः || ६-३२-१०
verses.translation:
"Even you who were actually intent on protecting me, fallen in great affliction and immersed 
in an ocean of grief, have been killed by the enemies"

verses.sanskrit:
सा श्वश्रूर् मम कौसल्या त्वया पुत्रेण राघव |

वत्सेन इव यथा धेनुर् विवत्सा वत्सला कृता || ६-३२-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. raaghava = O; Rama! saa kausalya = that Kausalya; mama 
= my; shvashruuH = mother in law; vatsalaa = who cherished you tenderly; kR^itaa 
= has been left; vivatsaa = without a son; tvayaa = by you; putreNa = the 
son; vatseneva = as by a calf; dhenu yathaa = as to the cow
verses.translation:
"O, Rama! My mother- in-law Kausalya who cherished you tenderly has been left without 
a son and resembles a cow that has lost its calf."

verses.sanskrit:
उदिष्टम् दीर्घम् आयुस् ते यैर् अचिन्त्य पराक्रम |

अनृतम् वचनम् तेषाम् अल्प आयुर् असि राघव || ६-३२-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. raaghava = O; Rama! UddishhTam = It was mentioned; daivaj^Nairapi 
= even by astrologers; te = (that) your; aayuH = life span; diirgham = is 
lengthy; raaghava = O;Rama! teshhaam = their; vachanam = words; anR^itam 
= are wrong; asi = you are; alpaayuH = short lived.
verses.translation:
"O, Rama! It was mentioned even by astrologers that your life span is lengthy. O, Rama! 
Their words are wrong. You are short lived."

verses.sanskrit:
अथ वा नश्यति प्रज्ना प्राज्नस्य अपि सतस् तव |

पचत्य् एनम् तथा कालो भूतानाम् प्रभवो ह्ययम् || ६-३२-१३
verses.translation:
"Otherwise, your prudence might have disappeared even though you were sagacious, for, 
Time the master of all beings has brought you to an end."

verses.sanskrit:
अदृष्टम् मृत्युम् आपन्नः कस्मात् त्वम् नय शास्त्रवित् |

व्यसनानाम् उपायज्नः कुशलो ह्यसि वर्जने || ६-३२-१४
verses.translation:
"How is it possible that you, who knew the doctrine of political ethics obtained an unforeseen 
death? You, who knew the science of expediency, were indeed skilled in warding off calamities"

verses.sanskrit:
तथा त्वम् सम्परिष्वज्य रौद्रया अतिनृशंसया |

काल रात्र्या मया आच्चिद्य हृतः कमल लोचन || ६-३२-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. kamala lochana = O;Rama; the lotus eyed! kaalaraatryaaH = 
by the night of all destroying time; ati nR^ishamsayaa = which was very much cruel; roudrayaa 
= and terrible; tathaa(you were)thus; samparishhvajya = encircled ; hR^itaH = embezzled;
aachchhidya = and snatched away; mama = from me.
verses.translation:
"O, Rama, the lotus eyed! The night of destroying time which was very much cruel and 
terrible thus has encircled you, embezzled you and snatched you away from me."

verses.sanskrit:
उपशेषे महा बाहो माम् विहाय तपस्विनीम् |

प्रियाम् इव शुभाम् नारीम् पृथिवीम् पुरुष ऋषभ || ६-३२-१६
verses.translation:
"O, the mighty armed! O, the best of men! Leaving the miserable me, you lied down here, 
embracing the earth as your beloved"

verses.sanskrit:
अर्चितम् सततम् यत्नाद् गन्ध माल्यैर् मया तव |

इदम् ते मत् प्रियम् वीर धनुः कान्चन भूषितम् || ६-३२-१७
verses.translation:
"O, valiant Rama! Here is your bow which was decorated with gold, dear to me and worshipped 
diligently by you with sandal paste and wreath of flowers."

verses.sanskrit:
पित्रा दशरथेन त्वम् श्वशुरेण मम अनघ |

पूर्वैसः च पितृभिः सार्धम् नूनम् स्वर्गे समागतः || ६-३२-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. anagha = O; the faultless Rama!; evam = you; nuunam 
= surely; samaagataH = have met; svarge = in heaven; mama = my; shvashureNa 
= father in law; pitraa = and your father; dasharathena = Dasaratha; sarvaiH 
= and all; pitR^ibhiHsaartham = multitude of manes.
verses.translation:
"O, the faultless Rama! Surely, you must have met in heaven your father and my father-in- 
law Dasaratha as well as all the multitude of manes."

verses.sanskrit:
दिवि नक्षत्र भूतस् त्वम् महत् कर्म कृतम् प्रियम् |

पुण्यम् राज ऋषि वंशम् त्वम् आत्मनः समुपेक्षसे || ६-३२-१९
verses.translation:
"You have abandoned your own pious lineage of royal sages but whereas you performed a 
great act, which illuminated the heaven like a luminary."

verses.sanskrit:
किम् मान् न प्रेक्षसे राजन् किम् माम् न प्रतिभाषसे |

बालाम् बालेन सम्प्राप्ताम् भार्याम् माम् सह चारिणीम् || ६-३२-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. raajan = O; prince!; kim = why; naprakshhyase = 
don't you look ; maam = at me?; kim = why; na pratibhaashhase = don't you 
reply; maam = me; bhaaryaam = a wife; sahachaariNiim = who lived with you 
together; sampraaptam = since the time of my obtaining; baalaam = as girl; baalena 
= by you as a boy.
verses.translation:
"O, prince! Why don't you look at me? Why don't you reply me, a wife who lived with you 
together since the time of obtaining me as a girl by you as a boy."

verses.sanskrit:
संश्रुतम् गृह्णता पाणिम् चरिष्यामि इति यत् त्वया |

स्मर तन् मम काकुत्स्थ नय माम् अपि दुह्खिताम् || ६-३२-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. kaakutthsa = O; Rama! Smaranaama = remember; tat 
= that; yat = which; samshrutam = was promised; tvayaa = by you; paaNim 
gR^ihNataa = at the time of our marriage; charishhyaami iti = I shall have righteousconduct 
with you; naya = take; maamapi = me also with you; duHkhitaam = wretched 
as I am.
verses.translation:
"O, Rama! Remember your solemn promise at the time of our marriage, saying I shall have 
righteous conduct with you' Take me also with you, wretched as I Am."

verses.sanskrit:
कस्मान् माम् अपहाय त्वम् गतो गतिमताम् वर |

अस्माल् लोकाद् अमुम् लोकम् त्यक्त्वा माम् इह दुह्खिताम् || ६-३२-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. vara = O; the best; gatimataam = among the beings; kasmaat 
= for what reason; tvam = you; gataH = have gone; apahaaya = quitting;
maam = me? Tyaktvaa = leaving; maamapi = even me; duHkhitaam = the miserable 
woman;( why have you gone); amum = to that; lokam = world; asmaat = from 
this; lokaat = world?
verses.translation:
"O, Rama the best of beings! Why have you gone away, quitting me like this? Leaving me, 
the miserable woman, why have you gone to the other world from this world?"

verses.sanskrit:
कल्याणैर् उचितम् यत् तत् परिष्वक्तम् मया एव तु |

क्रव्य अदैस् तत् शरीरम् ते नूनम् विपरिकृष्यते || ६-३२-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. kravyaadaiH = by wild beasts; tat = that; shariiram 
= body; nuunam = is now; viparikR^ishhyate = being dragged about; ruchiram 
= the beautiful; gaatram = body; parishhvaktam = was cuddled; mayaiva = by 
me; kalyaaNaiH = with divine essences.
verses.translation:
"That beautiful body, which was cuddled by me with divine essences, is now being dragged 
about by wild beasts."

verses.sanskrit:
अग्निष्तोम आदिभिर् यज्नैर् इष्टवान् आप्त दक्षिणैः |

अग्नि होत्रेण संस्कारम् केन त्वम् तु न लप्स्यसे || ६-३२-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. kena = why are you; na tu lapsyase = not securing; samskaaram 
= the privilege of being cremated; agnihotreNa = with sacrificial fire; (even though); 
tvam = you; ishhTavaan = have worshipped the Lord; yajN^aiH = through sacrificial 
performances; agnishhTomaadibhiH = such as agnishtoma; aaptadakshhiNaiH = furnished 
with abundant gifts?
verses.translation:
"Why are you not securing the privilege of being cremated with a sacrificial fire, even 
though you have worshipped the Lord through sacrificial performances such as Agnishtoma, duly furnished 
with abundant gifts?".

verses.sanskrit:
प्रव्रज्याम् उपपन्नानाम् त्रयाणाम् एकम् आगतम् |

परिप्रक्ष्यति कौसल्या लक्ष्मणम् शोक लालसा || ६-३२-२५
verses.translation:
"Kausalya absorbed as she was with grief can only see Lakshmana alone returned out of 
the three who attained exile."

verses.sanskrit:
स तस्याः परिपृच्चन्त्या वधम् मित्र बलस्य ते |

तव च आख्यास्यते नूनम् निशायाम् राक्षसैर् वधम् || ६-३२-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. saH = he; aakhyaasyate = will narrate; nuunam = 
surely; tasyaaH = to her; paripR^ichchhantyaaH = when enquired; tava = about 
your; vadhamcha = slaghter; vadham = as also the killing; raakshhasaiH = 
by the demons; te mitra balasya = of the army of your ally; nishaayaam = at night.
verses.translation:
"He will narrate surely to her when enquired, about your slaughter as also the killing 
of the army of your alley, by demons at night."

verses.sanskrit:
सा त्वाम् सुप्तम् हतम् श्रुत्वा माम् च रक्षो गृहम् गताम् |

हृदयेन विदीर्णेन न भविष्यति राघव || ६-३२-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. raaghava = O; Rama! Shrutvaa = hearing; tvaam = 
that you; hatam = having been killed; suptam = while sleeping; maam = and 
me; gataam = having obtained; rakshhogR^iham = the house of ogres; saa = 
she; na bhavishhyati = will be no more; hR^idayana = with her heart; avadiirNena 
= broken.
verses.translation:
"O, Rama! Hearing that you were killed while sleeping and that I was kept in the house 
of ogres, she will be no more, as her heart will be broken."

verses.sanskrit:
मम हेतोरनार्याया अवघः पार्थिवात्मजः |

रामः सागमुत्तीर्य वीर्यवान् गोष्पदे हतः || ६-३२-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. raamaH = Rama; anaghaH = the faultless man; paarthivaatmajaH 
= the prince; viiryavaan = and the strong man; hataH = was killed; goshhpade; in a 
small puddle; uttiirya = after crossing; saagaram = the ocean; hetoH = for 
the cause; mama = of me; anaaryaayaaH = an unworthy woman.
verses.translation:
"The strong and faultless prince Rama was killed in a small puddle, after crossing the 
ocean, for the cause of me, an unworthy woman."

verses.sanskrit:
अहम् दाशरथेनोढा मोहात्स्वकुपांसनी |

आर्यपुत्रस्य रामस्य भार्या मृत्युरजायत || ६-३२-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. aham- I; svakulapaamsanii = the obloquy of my race; uuDhaa 
= was wedded; daasharathena = by Rama; mohaat = due to ignorance; bhaaryaa 
= a wife herself; ajaayata = proved to be; mR^ityuH = the death; raamasya 
= of Rama; aaryaputrasya = an honorable man.
verses.translation:
"I, the obloquy of my race, was wedded by Rama due to ignorance. Thus, a wife herself 
has eventually proved to be the cause for death of Rama, an honorable man."

verses.sanskrit:
मानमाव्याम् मया जातिम् वारितम् दानमुत्तमम् |

याहमद्येह शोचामि भार्या सर्वातिथेरपि || ६-३२-३०
verses.translation:
"Even though I was a wife of Rama who showed hospitality to all the guests, it is I, 
that very person, who is weeping here and now, because without doubt, in a previous existence, an excellent 
gift was refused by me (to an eligible person)."

verses.sanskrit:
साधु पातय माम् क्षिप्रम् रामस्य उपरि रावणः |

समानय पतिम् पत्न्या कुरु कल्याणम् उत्तमम् || ६-३२-३१
verses.wordMeaning:
31. raavaNa = O; Ravana! samaanaya = unite; patnaya 
= the wife; patim = with the husband; kshhipram = and without delay; ghaataya 
= arrange to kill; maam = me; saadhu = right; upari = at the head; raamasya 
= of Rama; kuru = perform; uttamam = an admirable; kalyaaNam = and auspicious 
act
verses.translation:
"Unite the wife with the husband and without delay, arrange to kill me right at the head 
of Rama. Thus, you will perform an admirable and auspicious act."

verses.sanskrit:
शिरसा मे शिरसः च अस्य कायम् कायेन योजय |

रावण अनुगमिष्यामि गतिम् भर्तुर् महात्मनः || ६-३२-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. raavaNa = O; Ravana! yojaya = join; me shirasaa 
= my head; asya = with this Rama's; shiraH = head; kaayam = body; kaayena 
= with the body; anugamishhyaami = I shall go along ; gatim = the path; mahaatmanaH 
= of my high soled; bhartuH = Lord.
verses.translation:
"O, Ravana! Join my head with his head and my body with his body. I shall go along the 
path of my magnanimous Lord."

verses.sanskrit:
इति सा दुह्ख सम्तप्ता विललाप आयत ईक्षणा |

भर्तुः शिरो धनुस् तत्र समीक्ष्य जनक आत्मजा || ६-३२-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. aayatekshhaNaa = the wide eyed Sita; samiikshhya = seeing;
tatra = there; bhartuH = her husband's; shiraH = head; dhanushchaiva 
= and the bow; punaH punaH = again and again; duHkha santaptaa = was tormented with 
grief; vilalapa = and lamented; itiiva = in this way.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् लालप्यमानायाम् सीतायाम् तत्र राक्षसः |

अभिचक्राम भर्तारम् अनीकस्थः क्ऱ्त अन्जलिः || ६-३२-३४
verses.wordMeaning:
34. siitaayaam = while Seetha; evam = was thus; laalapyamanayaam 
= weeping; raakshhasaH = a demon; aniikasthaH = who was a royal guard; abhichakraama 
= approached; bhartaaram = his Lord; tatra = there; kR^ItaaN^jaliH = with 
his joined palms in salutation. (And cried as follows):

verses.sanskrit:
विजयस्व आर्य पुत्र इति सो अभिवाद्य प्रसाद्य च |

न्यवेदयद् अनुप्राप्तम् प्रहस्तम् वाहिनी पतिम् || ६-३२-३५
verses.wordMeaning:
35. vijayasva = May you be victorious; aaryaputra = O; Noble 
Lord! saH = he; abhivaadya = respectfully announced; iti = thus; prasaadyacha 
= got propitiated; nyavedayat = and informed; prahastam = that Prahasta; vaahiniipatim 
= the army chief; anupraaptam = had come.
verses.translation:
"May you be victorious, O noble Lord!" he respectfully announced thus, thereby winning 
his pleasure and informed that Prahasta the army chief had come.

verses.sanskrit:
अमात्यैः स हितः सर्वैः प्रहस्तस्त्वामुपस्थितः |

तेन दर्शनकामेन अहम् प्रस्थापितः प्रभो || ६-३२-३६
verses.wordMeaning:
36. prabho = O; Lord!; prahastaH = Prahasta; upasthitaH 
= has come; sahitaH = along with; sarvaH = all; amaatyaiH = ministers;
darshana kaamena = eager as he is to see; tvaam = you; aham = I; prasthaapitaH 
= have been sent; tena = by him.
verses.translation:
"O, Lord! Prahasta has come with all ministers. He has sent me, eager as he is, to see 
you."

verses.sanskrit:
मानमस्ति महारा ज राजभावात् क्षमान्वित |

किंचिद् आत्ययिकम् कार्यम् तेषाम् त्वम् दर्शनम् कुरु || ६-३२-३७
verses.translation:
"O, king, endowed with patience! There is a little urgent work by way of king's duty. 
Please accord them your audience now."

verses.sanskrit:
एतत् श्रुत्वा दशग्रीवो राक्षस प्रतिवेदितम् |

अशोक वनिकाम् त्यक्त्वा मन्त्रिणाम् दर्शनम् ययौ || ६-३२-३८
verses.wordMeaning:
38. shrutvaa = hearing; etat = the aforesaid; raakshhasa 
prativeditam = communication of the demon; dashagriivaH = Ravana; tyaktvaa = 
left; ashokavanikaam = the garden of Ashoka; yayou = and accorded; darshanam 
= his audience; mantriNaam = to the ministers.

verses.sanskrit:
स तु सर्वम् समर्थ्य एव मन्त्रिभिः क्ऱ्त्यम् आत्मनः |

सभाम् प्रविश्य विदधे विदित्वा राम विक्रमम् || ६-३२-३९
verses.wordMeaning:
39. saH = he; samarthaiva = deliberated; aatmanaH = 
with his; mantribhiH = ministers; sarvam = about the entire things; kR^ityam 
= to be done; pravishya = entered; sabhaam = the council chamber; viditvaa 
= and understanding; raamavikramam = about the strength of the Rama; vidhadhe = arranged 
for what was to be done.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्तर्धानम् तु तत् शीर्षम् तच् च कार्मुकम् उत्तमम् |

जगाम रावणस्य एव निर्याण समनन्तरम् || ६-३२-४०

राक्षस इन्द्रस् तु तैः सार्धम् मन्त्रिभिर् भीम विक्रमैः |

समर्थयाम् आस तदा राम कार्य विनिश्चयम् || ६-३२-४१

अविदूर स्थितान् सर्वान् बल अध्यक्षान् हित एषिणः |

अब्रवीत् काल सद्ऱ्शो रावणो राक्षस अधिपः || ६-३२-४२

शीघ्रम् भेरी निनादेन स्फुट कोण आहतेन मे |

समानयध्वम् सैन्यानि वक्तव्यम् च न कारणम् || ६-३२-४३
verses.translation:
"By a sound of a drum beaten audibly with a drum stick, immediately summon all my forces. 
Do not tell them the reason for which I am calling."

verses.sanskrit:
ततस् तथा इति प्रतिगृह्य तद् वचो |

स्तदैव दूताः सहसा महाद्बलम् |

समानयंसः चैव समागतम् च ते |

न्यवेदयन् भर्तरि युद्ध कान्क्षिणि || ६-३२-४४

sargas.title: Sarga 33
sargas.introduction:
An ogre named Sarama reassures Seetha by telling her that Ravana, the conjurer
created an illusory appearance of Rama's head and exhibited it before her. She informs Seetha that
Rama is still alive and is stationed along with his forces at the southern shore of the ocean.
Sarama restores confidence to Seetha, by saying that Rama after killing Ravana in battle will soon
win her back.

verses.sanskrit:
सीताम् तु मोहिताम् दृष्ट्वा सरमा नाम राक्षसी |

आससाद आशु वैदेहीम् प्रियाम् प्रणयिनी सखी || ६-३३-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. atha = then; dR^ishhTvaa = seeing; mahitaam =
the bewildered; siitaam = Seetha; raakshhasii = a demon; sarama = called
Sarama; praNayinii = an affectionate; sakhii = female companion;
aasasaada = approached; priyaam = her best loved; vaidehiim =
Seetha.

verses.sanskrit:
मोहिताम् राक्षसेन्द्रेण सीताम् परमदुःखिताम् |

आश्वासयामास तदा सरमा मृदुभाषिणी || ६-३३-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. tadaa = then; saramaa = Sarama; mR^idu
bhaashiNii = the soft-spoken woman; aashvaasayaamaasa = consoled; siitaam =
Seetha; mohitaam = who was infatuated; raakshhasendreNa = by Ravana;
paramaduHkhitaam = and who was very much in grief.

verses.sanskrit:
सा हि तत्र कृता मित्रम् सीतया रक्ष्यमाणया |

रक्षन्ती रावणाद् इष्टा सानुक्रोशा दृढ व्रता || ६-३३-३

सा ददर्श सखीम् सीताम् सरमा नष्ट चेतनाम् |

उपावृत्य उत्थिताम् ध्वस्ताम् वडवाम् इव पांसुषु || ६-३३-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. saa = that; saramaa = Sarama; sakhii = the
companion; dadarsha = saw; siitaam = Seetha; nashhTachetanaam = who lost
her consciousness; upaavR^itya = having rolled herself; paamsushhu = in dust;
badabaamiva = like a female horse; utthitaam = risen; dhvastaam =
covered with dust

verses.sanskrit:
ताम् समाश्वासयाम् आस सखी स्नेहेन सुव्रता |

उक्ता यद् रावणेन त्वम् प्रत्युक्तम् च स्वयम् त्वया || ६-३३-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. sakhii = that companion; Sarama; snehena = affectionate;
samaa shvaasayaamaasa = consoled; taam = that Seetha; suvrataam = the
very virtuous woman; vaidehi = O; Seetha; samaashvasihi = be comfortable; maa
bhuut = Let there not be; vyathaa = perturbation; te maanasaa = in your
mind.

verses.sanskrit:
उक्ता यद्रावणेन त्वम् प्रत्युक्तश्च स्वयम् त्वया |

सखी स्नेहेन तद् भीरु मया सर्वम् प्रतिश्रुतम् || ६-३३-६

लीनया गनहे शूह्ये भयम् उत्सृज्य रावणात् |

तव हेतोर् विशाल अक्षि न हि मे जीवितम् प्रियम् || ६-३३-७

स सम्भ्रान्तश च निष्क्रान्तो यत् कृते राक्षस अधिपः |

तच् च मे विदितम् सर्वम् अभिनिष्क्रम्य मैथिलि || ६-३३-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. maithili = O;Seetha! Sarvam = everything; viditam = was
known; me = to me; abhinishhkramya = having gone outside; tatra = about
that matter; yatkR^ite = why; saH raakshhaseshvaraH = that Ravana;
nishhkaantah = had gone away; sambhraantaH = agitated.
verses.translation:
"O, Seetha! Every thing was ascertained by me on the spot, after going out, about
the reason for which Ravana has gone away agitated."

verses.sanskrit:
न शक्यम् सौप्तिकम् कर्तुम् रामस्य विदित आत्मनः |

वधश्च पुरुष व्याघ्रे तस्मिन्न् एव उपपद्यते || ६-३३-९
verses.translation:
"It is not possible to attack Rama, a man of self awareness, while he is asleep. Nay
even killing him, the tiger among men, is well nigh impossible."

verses.sanskrit:
न च एव वानरा हन्तुम् शक्याः पादप योधिनः |

सुरा देव ऋषभेण इव रामेण हि सुरक्षिताः || ६-३३-१०
verses.translation:
"It is not possible to kill in this manner even monkeys, who perform fighting with
trees, in as much as they are fully protected by Rama, even as celestials are protected by Indra
their lord"

verses.sanskrit:
दीर्घ वृत्त भुजह् श्रीमान् महा उरस्कह् प्रतापवान् |

धन्वी सम्हनन उपेतो धर्म आत्मा भुवि विश्रुतः || ६-३३-११

विक्रान्तो रक्षिता नित्यम् आत्मनश्च परस्य च |

लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा कुशली नय शास्त्रवित् || ६-३३-१२

हन्ता पर बल ओघानाम् अचिन्त्य बल पौरुषः |

न हतो राघवः श्रीमान् सीते शत्रु निबर्हणः || १३
verses.translation:
"O, Seetha! Rama, who is endowed with long and well rounded arms, an illustrious
man, who is large-chested, a man of great energy, an archer well known in the world, a man endowed
with muscularity, a righteous minded man, a person of celebrity on earth, a man of eternal
strength, a protector for himself and for others, while together with his brother Lakshmana, a man
of high descent, a knower of doctrines of conduct and behavior, the killer of a stream of enemy
forces, a man of inconceivable strength and valor, a venerable man and an annihilator of
adversaries, has not been killed."

verses.sanskrit:
अयुक्त बुद्धि कृत्येन सर्व भूत विरोधिना |

इयम् प्रयुक्ता रौद्रेण माया मायाविदा त्वयि || ६-३३-१४
verses.translation:
"A trickery has been hurled on you here by the cruel Ravana, a conjurer, who is
perverse in thinking and action and an enemy of all beings."

verses.sanskrit:
शोकस् ते विगतः सर्वः कल्याणम् त्वाम् उपस्थितम् |

ध्रुवम् त्वाम् भजते लक्ष्मीः प्रियम् प्रीति करम् शृणु || ६-३३-१५
verses.translation:
"Your sorrow is (now) gone once for all. An all-pervading auspiciousness will await
you. Accordingly prosperity will attend on you. Hear me."

verses.sanskrit:
उत्तीर्य सागरम् रामः सह वानर सेनया |

सम्निविष्टः समुद्रस्य तीरम् आसाद्य दक्षिणम् || ६-३३-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. raamaH = Rama; vaanarasenayaa saha = along with his army
of monkeys; uttiirya = having crossed; saagaram = the ocean; aasaadya =
reached; dakshhiNam tiiram = the southern shore; samudrasya = of the sea;
samnivishhTaH = and is encamped; (there)
verses.translation:
"Rama, along with his army of monkeys, having crossed the ocean, reached its
southern shore and is encamped there."

verses.sanskrit:
दृष्टो मे परिपूर्ण अर्थः काकुत्स्थः सह लक्ष्मणः |

सहितैः सागर अन्तस्थैर् बलैस् तिष्ठति रक्षितः || ६-३३-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. kaakutthsaH = Rama; saha lakshmaNaH = who was along with
Lakshmana; paripuurNaarthaH = who was full of meaning; dR^ishhTati = was
perceived; me = by me; tishhTati = who is stationed (there); rakshhitaH
= being protected; balaiH = by his forces; sahitaiH = who are united;
saagaraantasthaiH = near the ocean.
verses.translation:
"Rama, who was along with fully meaningful Lakshmana, was perceived by me. He stands
protected by the troops gathered together at the vicinity of the ocean."

verses.sanskrit:
अनेन प्रेषिता ये च राक्षसा लघु विक्रमः |

राघवस् तीर्णैत्य् एवम् प्रवृत्तिस् तैर् इह आहृता || ६-३३-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. anena = by him; ye = which; laghuvikramaaH =
quick paced; raakshhasaH = demons; preshhitaaH = were sent; pravR^ittiH
= the news; aahR^itaa = has been brought; taiH = by them; ityevam =
that; raaghavaH = Rama; tiirNaH = had crossed(the ocean.)
verses.translation:
"Some swift paced demons, who were dispatched by Ravana, brought this very news here
that Rama has crossed the ocean."

verses.sanskrit:
स ताम् श्रुत्वा विशाल अक्षि प्रवृत्तिम् राक्षस अधिपः |

एष मन्त्रयते सर्वैः सचिवैः सह रावणः || ६-३३-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. vishaalaakshhi = O; the wide-eyed Seetha! shrutvaa =
hearing; taam = that; pravR^ittim = news; saH eshhaH = yonder;
raavaNaH = Ravana; mantrayate = is deliberating; sachivaih saha = with
his ministers.
verses.translation:
"O, the wide-eyed Seetha! Hearing that news, yonder Ravana is busy making
deliberations with all his ministers."

verses.sanskrit:
इति ब्रुवाणा सरमा राक्षसी सीतया सह |

सर्व उद्योगेन सैन्यानाम् शब्दम् शुश्राव भैरवम् || ६-३३-२०

दण्ड निर्घात वादिन्याः श्रुत्वा भेर्या महा स्वनम् |

उवाच सरमा सीताम् इदम् मधुर भाषिणी || ६-३३-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. shrutvaa = hearing; mahaasvanam = a loud sound;
bheryaaH = of a kettle drum; daN^Danirghaatavaadinyaa = made to sound like a
thunder storm with drum stick; saramaa = Sarama; madhura bhaashhiNii = who speaks
sweetly; uvaacha = spoke; siitam = to Seetha; idam = these words:

verses.sanskrit:
सम्नाह जननी ह्य् एषा भैरवा भीरु भेरिका |

भेरी नादम् च गम्भीरम् शृणु तोयद निस्वनम् || ६-३३-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. bhiiru = O; timid Seetha! Eshhaa = this;
bhairavaa = frightful; bherikaa = sound of kettle drum; samvaahajananii
= is indeed indicative of preparation for a war; shruNu = hear; gambhiiram = the
thundering; bheriinaadamcha = sound of the kettle drum; toyadaniH svanam =
resembling the sound of a lightening cloud.
verses.translation:
"O, timid Seetha! This frightful sound of a kettledrum is indeed indicative of
preparation for a war. Hear also the thundering crash of the kettle drums, which resembles the
rumbling of clouds"

verses.sanskrit:
कल्प्यन्ते मत्त मातम्गा युज्यन्ते रथ वाजिनः |

तत्र तत्र च सम्नद्धाः सम्पतन्ति पदातयः || ६-३३-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. matta maataN^gaaH = elephants in rut; kalpyante = are
being caparisoned for war; rathavaajinaH = the chariot horses; yujyante = are
being prepared for battle; sahasrashaH = thousands; turagaaruuDhaaH = of horse
men ; praasa hastaaH = with darts in their hands; dR^ishyante = are being
seen.
verses.translation:
"Elephants in rut are being caparisoned for war. Chariot horses are being prepared
for a battle. Thousands of horsemen carrying darts in their hands are seen."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्र तत्र च सन्नद्धाः सम्पतन्ति सहस्रशः |

आपूर्यन्ते राज मार्गाः सैन्यैर् अद्भुत दर्शनैः || ६-३३-२४

वेगवद्भिर् नदद्भिश्च तोय ओघैर् इव सागरः |
verses.wordMeaning:
24. sahasrashaH = thousands; samnaddhaaH = of warriors
equipped with armor; sampatanti = are marching quickly ; tatra tatra cha = here
and there. RaajamaargaaH = Royal high ways; aapuuryante = are filled;
adbhutadarshanaiH = with wonderfully looking; sainyaiH = warriors; saagaraH
iva = like a sea; toyoughaiH = with its flood of waters; nadadbhishcha =
with roaring sound; vegavadbhiH = and velocity.
verses.translation:
"Thousands of soldiers equipped with armor are marching quickly hither and thither.
Royal highways are filled with wonderfully looking warriors like a swift flowing sea with its flood
of waters making a roaring sound."

verses.sanskrit:
शास्त्राणाम् च प्रसन्नानाम् चर्मणाम् वर्मणाम् तथा || ६-३३-२५

रथ वाजि गजानाम् च भूषितानाम् च रक्षसाम् |

सम्भ्रमो रक्षसामेष हृषितानाम् तरस्विनाम् || ६-३३-२६

प्रभाम् विसृजताम् पश्य नाना वर्णाम् समुत्थिताम् |

वनम् निर्दहतो धर्मे यथा रूपम् विभावसोः || ६-३३-२७
verses.translation:
"See hurriedly, this sheen of polished weapons, shields and coats of mail sending
forth luster of many hues like the splendor of fire consuming a forest in summer. Here is seen the
flurry of chariots, horses and elephants following the lead of Ravana as well as of energetic
demons thrilled with joy."

verses.sanskrit:
घण्टानाम् शृणु निर्घोषम् रथानाम् शृणु निस्वनम् |

हयानाम् हेषमाणानाम् शृणु तूर्य ध्वनिम् यथा || ६-३३-२८

उद्यत आयुध हस्तानाम् राक्षस इन्द्र अनुयायिनाम् |

सम्भ्रमो रक्षसाम् एष तुमुलो लोम हर्षणः || ६-३३-२९
verses.translation:
"Hear the sound of bells of elephants. Hear the ratting of chariots. Hear the cry of
neighing horses, resembling the flourish of trumpets. Here is the bustle of demons, carrying
uplifted weapons in their hands; following the lead of Ravana. This bustling activity is causing
hair to stand erect through terror."

verses.sanskrit:
श्रीस् त्वाम् भजति शोकघ्नी रक्षसाम् भयम् आगतम् |

रामात् कमल पत्र अक्षि दैत्यानाम् इव वासवात् || ६-३३-३०

अवजित्य जित क्रोधस् तम् अचिन्त्य पराक्रमः |

रावणम् समरे हत्वा भर्ता त्वा अधिगमिष्यति || ६-३३-३१
verses.translation:
"A good fortune, which will bring your sorrow to an end, awaits you. A peril has
arrived for demons. The lotus eyed Rama, your husband, who has overcome his anger but having an
unimaginable strength will kill Ravana in battle and win you back"

verses.sanskrit:
विक्रमिष्यति रक्षह्सु भर्ता ते सह लक्ष्मणः |

यथा शत्रुषु शत्रुघ्नो विष्णुना सह वासवः || ६-३३-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. tebhartaa = your consort; sahalakshhmanaNaH = along with
Lakshmana; vikramishhyati = will show his strength; rakshhassu = over the demons;
yathaa vaasavaH = like Indra the god of celestials; vishhNunaa saha = along with
Vishnu; the lord of preservation; shatrughnaH = who annihilates the enemies; (showing his
strength over); shatrushhu = the adversaries.
verses.translation:
"Your consort along with Lakshmana will show his valor against the demons, like
Indra the lord of celestials along with Vishnu the lord of preservation, the annihilator of enemies
exhibited his strength over his adversaries."

verses.sanskrit:
आगतस्य हि रामस्य क्षिप्रम् अन्क गताम् सतीम् |

अहम् द्रक्ष्यामि सिद्ध अर्थाम् त्वाम् शत्रौ विनिपातिते || ६-३३-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. kshhipram = very soon; aham = I; drakshhyaami =
shall see; tvaam = you; aN^kaagataam satiim = mounted on the lap; tasya
= of Rama; aagatasya = (really) arrived; siddhaarthaam = your object of his
coming being fulfilled; shatrou = and the enemy; vinipaatite = fallen.
verses.translation:
"Very soon, I shall see you, mounted on the lap of Rama who actually arrived, your
object of his coming being fulfilled and the enemy fallen."

verses.sanskrit:
अश्रूण्य् आनन्दजानि त्वम् वर्तयिष्यसि शोभने |

समागम्य परिष्वक्ता तस्य उरसि महा उरसः || ६-३३-३४
verses.translation:
"O, Seetha! You will shed tears of joy on the bosom of that broad-chested Rama, re-
united with you and holding you clasped to his breast."

verses.sanskrit:
अचिरान् मोक्ष्यते सीते देवि ते जघनम् गताम् |

धृताम् एताम् बहून् मासान् वेणीम् रामो महा बलः || ६-३३-३५
verses.translation:
"Before long, O divine Seetha, that mighty Rama will loosen the one single plait
which has reached past your hips, which you have worn these many months."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्य दृष्ट्वा मुखम् देवि पूर्ण चन्द्रम् इव उदितम् |

मोक्ष्यसे शोकजम् वारि निर्मोकम् इव पन्नगी || ६-३३-३६
verses.wordMeaning:
36. devii = O; queen! DR^ishhTvaa = seeing; tasya =
his; mukham = face; uditam puurNachandram iva = looking like a rising full moon;
mokshhyase = you will renounce; vaari = your tears; shokajam = born of
grief; pannagii iva = like a female snake; nirmokam = (casts off) its slough.
verses.translation:
"O, Queen! Seeing his face, looking like a rising moon, you will renounce your tears
born of grief, like a female snake casts off its slough."

verses.sanskrit:
रावणम् समरे हत्वा नचिराद् एव मैथिलि |

त्वया समग्रम् प्रियया सुख अर्हो लप्स्यते सुखम् || ६-३३-३७
verses.translation:
"O, Seetha! Having killed Ravana in combat within no time, Rama (who deserves
facility); will enjoy happiness in the company of the beloved, namely yourself."

verses.sanskrit:
समागता त्वम् रामेण मोदिष्यसि महात्मना |

सुवर्षेण समायुक्ता यथा सस्येन मेदिनी || ६-३३-३८
verses.translation:
"O, queen! Seek refuge now with him - the sun that rolls towards Meru the highest
mountain, like a horse that makes its round rapidly. That sun is indeed the source of all
creatures."
verses.commentary:
© August 2004, K. M. K. Murthy

sargas.title: Sarga 34
sargas.introduction:
Seetha asks Sarama to go secretly to Ravana and get to know what he is doing as well
as report to her back about Ravana's decision about the matter of releasing her or continuing to
keep her captive. Sarama secretly hears the conversation of Ravana with his ministers and reports
to Seetha that Ravana is not inclined to set her free. Sarama consoles Seetha, saying that Rama
with his sharp arrows will kill Ravana and take her back to Ayodhya.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ ताम् जात सम्तापाम् तेन वाक्येन मोदिताम् |

सरमा ह्लादयाम् आस महीं दग्धामिवाम्भसा || ६-३४-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. atha = then; taam = to that Seetha; jaataa
samtaapam = who was overwhelmed with anguish;moditaam = was made delighted;tena vaakyena = by
those words;saramaa = Sarama; samaahlaadayaamaasa = conferred delight;ambhasaa iva = like
by rain;dagdhaam = the parched; mahiim = earth.

verses.sanskrit:
ततस् तस्या हितम् सख्याश् चिकीर्षन्ती सखी वचः |

उवाच काले कालज्ना स्मित पूर्व अभिभाषिणी || ६-३४-२

उत्सहेयम् अहम् गत्वा त्वद् वाक्यम् असित ईक्षणे |

निवेद्य कुशलम् रामे प्रतिच्चन्ना निवर्तितुम् || ६-३४-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. asitekshhaNe = O; black eyed lady!; aham utsaheyam = I am
able; nivedya = to carry a message; kushalam = of good will; tvadvaakyam
= and your words; raame = to Rama; gatvaa = by going to him;
pratichchhannaa = secretly; nivartitum = and to return.
verses.translation:
"O, black eyed lady! I am capable of carrying a message of your words and good will
to Rama and to return secretly."

verses.sanskrit:
न हि मे क्रममाणाया निरालम्बे विहायसि |

समर्थो गतिम् अन्वेतुम् पवनो गरुडो अपि वा || ६-३४-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. kramamaaNaayaaH = when I am journeying; vihaayasi = in
the sky; niraalambe = self-supported; pavanaH = the god of wind; garuDo api
vaa = or even Garuda the eagle; na samarthaH = is not capable; anvetum = to
follow; me = my; gatim = movement
verses.translation:
"When I am journeying in the sky self -supported, neither the god of wind nor Garuda
the eagle can follow my movement."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् ब्रुवाणाम् ताम् सीता सरमाम् पुनर् अब्रवीत् |

मधुरम् श्लक्ष्णया वाचा पूर्व शोक अभिपन्नया || ६-३४-५

समर्था गगनम् गन्तुम् अपि वा त्वम् रसा तलम् |

अवगच्चाम्य् अकर्तव्यम् कर्तव्यम् ते मद् अन्तरे || ६-३४-६
verses.translation:
"You are capable of going to heaven or to the penultimate subterranean region. Know
from me today the duty that has got to be performed by you for my sake."

verses.sanskrit:
मत् प्रियम् यदि कर्तव्यम् यदि बुद्धिः स्थिरा तव |

ज्नातुम् इच्चामि तम् गत्वा किम् करोति इति रावणः || ६-३४-७
verses.translation:
"If your intention is to act kindly towards me and your resolve is firm , I wish you
to go and know what Ravana is doing now."

verses.sanskrit:
स हि माया बलः क्रूरो रावणः शत्रु रावणः |

माम् मोहयति दुष्ट आत्मा पीत मात्रा इव वारुणी || ६-३४-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. saH = raavaNaH = that Ravana; maayaabalaH = equipped with
strength in the shape of conjuring tricks; shatru raavaNaH = who makes his enemies cry;
kruuraH = is cruel; dushhTaatmaa = and evil minded; mohayati = has
bemused; maam = me; vaaruNiiva = as spirituous liquor(bemuses one); piita
maatraa = the moment it is imbibed.
verses.translation:
"That cruel and evil minded Ravana, equipped with strength in the shape of conjuring
tricks, who makes his enemies cry has bemused me, as spirituous liquor bemuses one, the moment it
is imbibed."

verses.sanskrit:
तर्जापयति माम् नित्यम् भर्त्सापयति च असक्र्त् |

राक्षसीभिः सुघोराभिर् या माम् रक्षन्ति नित्यशः || ६-३४-९
verses.translation:
"He causes me to be threatened by words all the time by the most frightful ogresses,
who always surround me and insult me by their act repeatedly."

verses.sanskrit:
उद्विग्ना शन्किता च अस्मि न च स्वस्थम् मनो मम |

तद् भयाच् च अहम् उद्विग्ना;अशोक वनिकाम् गताः || ६-३४-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. aham = I; asmi = am; udvignaa = depressed in
mind; shaN^kitaacha = and distrustful; mama = My; manaH = mind; na
svastham = is not in its natural state; gataa = being in; ashoka vanakaam =
Ashoka grove; aham = I; udvignaa = am distressed ; tadbhayaat = because
of his fear.
verses.translation:
"I am depressed in mind and distrustful. My mind is not in its natural state.
Staying in Ashoka grove, I am distressed because of his fear."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि नाम कथा तस्य निश्चितम् वा अपि यद् भवेत् |

निवेदयेथाः सर्वम् तत् परो मे स्याद् अनुग्रहः ||६-३४-११
verses.translation:
"Report to me, all that is decided by him during his talk going on with his
ministers about the matter of releasing me or keeping me captive. It will be of great service to
me."

verses.sanskrit:
साप्येवम् ब्रुवतीम् सीताम् सरमा वल्गु भाषिणी |

उवाच वचनम् तस्याः स्पृशन्ती बाष्प विक्लवम् || ६-३४-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. saa = that; saramaapi = Sarama also; mR^idu
bhaashiNii = who speaks kindly words; spR^ishantii = touching (wiping);
tasyaaH = her; vadanam = face; bashhpaviklabam = which got moist with
tears; uvaacha = replied; siitaam = to Seetha; bruuvatiim = who was
speaking; evam = as aforesaid:

verses.sanskrit:
एष ते यद्य् अभिप्रायस् तस्माद् गच्चामि जानकि |

गृह्य शत्रोर् अभिप्रायम् उपाव्र्त्ताम् च पश्य माम् || ६-३४-१३
verses.translation:
"If your opinion is like this, I shall go on that account, O, Seetha! Having grasped
the feeling of the enemies, I shall return O, Seetha!"

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्त्वा ततो गत्वा समीपम् तस्य रक्षसः |

शुश्राव कथितम् तस्य रावणस्य समन्त्रिणः || ६-३४-१४

सा श्रुत्वा निश्चयम् तस्य निश्चयज्ना दुरात्मनः |

पुनर् एव अगमत् क्षिप्रम् अशोक वनिकाम् तदा || ६-३४-१५

सा प्रविष्टा पुनस् तत्र ददर्श जनक आत्मजाम् |

प्रतीक्षमाणाम् स्वाम् एव भ्रष्ट पद्माम् इव श्रियम् || ६-३४-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. saa = she; pravishhTaa = who entered; tatra =
there; dadarsha = saw; tataH = then; janakaatmajam = Seetha; shriyam
iva = who looked like Lakshmi ;the goddess of prosperity; bhrashhTa padmaam = bereft
of the lotus; pratiikshhamaaNaam = who was waiting; svaameva = for her alone.

verses.sanskrit:
ताम् तु सीता पुनः प्राप्ताम् सरमाम् वल्गु भाषिणीम् |

परिष्वज्य च सुस्निग्धम् ददौ च स्वयम् आसनम् || ६-३४-१७

इह आसीना सुखम् सर्वम् आख्याहि मम तत्त्वतः |

क्रूरस्य निश्चयम् तस्य रावणस्य दुरात्मनः || ६-३४-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. aasiinaa = sitting; iha = here; sukham =
comfortably; aakhyaahi = tell; mama = me; tattvataH = really;
sarvam = about all; nishchayam = the design; tasya raavaNasya = of that
Ravana; kruurasya = the cruel; duraatmanaH = and the wicked demon.
verses.translation:
"Sitting here comfortably, tell me about the real design of that cruel and wicked
Ravana"

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्ता तु सरमा सीतया वेपमानया |

कथितम् सर्वम् आचष्त रावणस्य समन्त्रिणः || ६-३४-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. saramaa = (while) Sarama;uktaa = was enquired; evam =
thus; siitasya = by Seetha; vepamaanayaa = tremblingwith fear; aachashTa
= narrated; sarvam = all; kathitam = the conversation; raavaNasya = of
Ravana; samantriNaH = together with his ministers.

verses.sanskrit:
जनन्या राक्षस इन्द्रो वै त्वन् मोक्ष अर्थम् बृहद् वचः |

अविद्धेन च वैदेहि मन्त्रि वृद्धेन बोधितः || ६-३४-२०
verses.translation:
"Excellent words were spoken by Kaikasi, the mother of Ravana as well as Aviddha the
aged minister asking for your release, O Seetha!"

verses.sanskrit:
दीयताम् अभिसत्क्र्त्य मनुज इन्द्राय मैथिली |

निदर्शनम् ते पर्याप्तम् जन स्थाने यद् अद्भुतम् || ६-३४-२१
verses.translation:
"Let Seetha, be restored honorably to Rama the Lord of men. That wonderful** thing
happened in Janasthana is an enough eye- opener to you."
verses.commentary:
** Rama exterminated fourteen thousand demons led by such great warriors as
Khara and Dushana at Janastana.

verses.sanskrit:
लन्घनम् च समुद्रस्य दर्शनम् च हनूमतः |

वधम् च रक्षसाम् युद्धे कः कुर्यान् मानुषो भुवि || ६-३४-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. kaH = what; maanushhaH = mortal; kuryaat =
could accomplish; laN^ghanam = the crossing; samudrasya = of the ocean;
hanuumataH = by Hanuman; vadhamcha = and the carnage; rakshhasaam = of
the demons; yuddhe = in combat; bhuvi = on the earth?
verses.translation:
"Which mortal would accomplish the crossing of the ocean, the discovery of Seetha by
Hanuman, and the carnage of the demons in combat on this earth?"

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् स मन्त्रि वृद्धैश्च मात्रा च बहु भाषितः |

न त्वाम् उत्सहते मोक्तुम् अर्तह्म् अर्थ परो यथा || ६-३४-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. bodhitaH = though admonished; bahu = in many ways;
evam = thus; mantrivR^iddhaishcha = by the aged ministers; maatraacha =
and by his mother; saH = he; na utsahate = does not want; moktum = to
release; tvaam = you; arthamiva = as with riches; arthaparaH = by a
miser.
verses.translation:
"Though admonished in many ways by the aged ministers and his mother he is not
inclined to set you free, any more than a miser would leave his hold on his riches.

verses.sanskrit:
न उत्सहत्य् अम्र्तो मोक्तुम् युद्धे त्वाम् इति मैथिलि |

सामात्यस्य नृशंसस्य निश्चयो ह्य् एष वर्तते || ६-३४-२४
verses.translation:
"O, Seetha! He does not want to release you, without dying himself in a battle. This
is the resolve of the cruel Ravana along with his ministers."

verses.sanskrit:
तद् एषा सुस्थिरा बुद्धिर्मृत्यु लोभाद् उपस्थिता |

भयान् न शक्तस् त्वाम् मोक्तुम् अनिरस्तस् तु सम्युगे || ६-३४-२५

राक्षसानाम् च सर्वेषाम् आत्मनश् च वधेन हि |

निहत्य रावणम् सम्ख्ये सर्वथा निशितैः शरैः || ६-३४-२६

प्रतिनेष्यति रामस् त्वाम् अयोध्याम् असित ईक्षणे |
verses.translation:
"O, dark eyed Seetha! Killing Ravana by his sharp arrows in combat, Rama will take
you back to Ayodhya by all means."

verses.sanskrit:
एतस्मिन्न् अन्तरे शब्दो भेरी शन्ख समाकुलः || ६-३४-२७

श्रुतो वै सर्व सैन्यानाम् कम्पयन् धरणी तलम् |

श्रुत्वा तु तम् वानर सैन्य शब्दम् |

लन्का गता राक्षस राज भ्र्त्याः |

नष्ट ओजसो दैन्य परीत चेष्टाः |

श्रेयो न पश्यन्ति न्र्पस्य दोषैः || ६-३४-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. Hearing that sound of that monkey troops; the servants of Ravana stationed
in Lanka were lack- luster with their movements; overcome by depression. They did not see anything
salutary in it; owing to the fault of their king. wM-shrutvaa = hearing; tvam =
that; vaanara sainya naadam = sound of the monkey troops; raakshhasa
raajabhR^ityaaH = the servants of Ravana; laN^kaagataaH = employed in Lanka;
hatoujasaH = were bereft of luster; dainyapariitacheshhTaaH = with their
movements overcome by depression; na pashyanti = they did not see; shreyaH =
anything salutary; doshhaat = owing to the fault; nR^ipashya = of their king.

sargas.title: Sarga 35
sargas.introduction:
With the sound of kettle drums and couches, Rama arrives to Lanka with his army.
Ravana hears those sounds and calls his ministers to chalk out his further course of action.
Malyavan, Ravana's maternal grandfather advises Ravana to conclude peace with Rama and restore
Seetha to him as he is perceiving adverse omens.

verses.sanskrit:
तेन शन्ख विमिश्रेण भेरी शब्देन राघवः |

उपयतो महा बाहू रामह् पर पुरम् जयः || ६-३५-१

तम् निनादम् निशम्य अथ रावणो राक्षस ईश्वरः |

मुहूर्तम् ध्यानम् आस्थाय सचिवान् अभ्युदैक्षत ||६-३५-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. nishamya = hearing; atha = thereafter; tam =
that; ninaadam = sound; raavaNaH = Ravana; raakSaseshvaraH = the lord of
demons; aasthaaya = resorting to dhyaanam = meditation; muhuurtam = for a moment;
abhyudaikSata = looked towards; sachivaan = his ministers.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ तान् सचिवांस् तत्र सर्वान् आभाष्य रावणः |

सभाम् सम्नादयन् सर्वाम् इत्य् उवाच महा बलः || ६-३५-३

जगत्पम्तापनः क्रूरोगर्हयन् राक्षसेश्वरः |

तरणम् सागरस्य अपि विक्रमम् बल संचयम् || ६-३५-४

यद् उक्तवन्तो रामस्य भवन्तस् तन् मया श्रुतम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
4. shrutam = It was heard; mayaa = by me; tat =
that; yat = which; uktavantaH = was told; bhavantaH = by you;
raamasya = of Rama's; taraNam = crossing; asya saagarasya = of this
ocean; vikramam = his prowess; balapauruSam = strength and heroism.
verses.translation:
"I have heard what you told me about Rama's crossing the ocean, his prowess,
strength and heroism."

verses.sanskrit:
भवतश्चाप्यहम् वेद्मि युद्धे सत्यपराक्रमान् || ६-३५-५

तूष्णीकानीक्षतोन्योन्यम् विदित्वाम् रामविक्रमम् |
verses.translation:
"I know that you are truly brave in the battle-field, yet, on beholding that valiant
Rama, you too look on each other in silence."

verses.sanskrit:
ततस् तु सुमहा प्राज्नो माल्यवान् नाम राक्षसः || ६-३५-६

रावणस्य वचः श्रुत्वा मातुः पैतामहो अब्रवीत् |
verses.wordMeaning:
6. shrutvaa = hearing; vachaH = the words;
raavaNasya = of Ravana; raakSasaH = a demon; maalyavaan naama = called
Malyavan; maataamahaH = his maternal grandfather; sumahaapraajJNaH = the highly
intelligent; tataH = then; abraviit = answered; iti = as follows:

verses.sanskrit:
विद्यास्वभिविनीतो यो राजा राजन् नय अनुगः || ६-३५-७

स शास्ति चिरम् ऐश्वर्यम् अरींश्च कुरुते वशे |
verses.translation:
"O, king! He who is well-versed in (fourteen)* sciences and follows the path of
prudence, enjoys sovereignty for a long time and compels his enemies too into subjugation."
verses.commentary:
Four Vedas, six Vedaangas, the Puranas, the Mimamsa, Nyaya and Dharma together
constitute fourteen sciences.

verses.sanskrit:
संदधानो हि कालेन विगृह्णंसः चारिभिः सह || ६-३५-८

स्व पक्ष वर्धनम् कुर्वन् महद् ऐश्वर्यम् अश्नुते ||
verses.wordMeaning:
8. (He who); samdadhaanaH = his indded concludes peace; aribhiH
saha = even with enemies; vigR^ihNancha = or wages war; kaalena = at a
fitting tiem; kurvan vardhanam = strengthens svapakSe = his own party; ashnute =
and attains; mahat = a great; aishvaryam = power.
verses.translation:
"He who concludes peace even with enemies or wages war at a fitting time strengthens
his own party and attains a great power."

verses.sanskrit:
हीयमानेन कर्तव्यो राज्ना संधिः समेन च || ६-३५-९

न शत्रुम् अवमन्येत ज्यायान् कुर्वीत विग्रहम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
9. samdhiH = A treaty of peace; kartavyaH = should be
reached; raajN^aaH = by a king; hiiyamaanena = who is weaker; samena cha
= or equal; na avamanyeta = He should not under rate; shatrum = that enemy;
jyaayaan = (A king who is) stronger; kurrviita = should make; vigraham =
war.
verses.translation:
"A treaty of peace should be reached by a king who is weaker or equal to an enemy.
The king should never underrate that enemy. If the king is more powerful, he should make war on the
enemy."

verses.sanskrit:
तन्मह्यम् रोचते संधिः सह रामेण रावण || ६-३५-१०

यद् अर्थम् अभियुक्ताः स्म सीता तस्मै प्रदीयताम् |
verses.translation:
"O, Ravana! For that reason, alliance with Rama finds favour with me. Let Seetha,
for whose sake you are being attacked, be restored to him."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्य देव ऋषयः सर्वे गन्धर्वासः च जय एषिणः || ६-३५-११

विरोधम् मा गमस् तेन संधिस् ते तेन रोचताम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
11. sarve = all; devarSayaH = the celestials; sages;
gandharvaashcha = and Gandharvas the celestial musicians; jayaiSiNaH = wish for
victory; tasya = of that Rama; maagamaH- do not get; virodham- enmity; tena =
with them; samdhiH = (Let) alliance; tena = with him; rochataam = be
agreable; te = to you.
verses.translation:
"All the celestials, sages and Gandharvas the celestial musicians are wishing for
victory of Rama. Do not get an enmity with them. Let alliance with him be acceptable to you."

verses.sanskrit:
असृजद् भगवान् पक्षौ द्वाव् एव हि पितामहः || ६-३५-१२

सुराणाम् असुराणाम् च धर्म अधर्मौ तद् आश्रयौ |
verses.translation:
"Brahma, the lord of creation, created only two classes of beings, the celestials
and the demons. Celestials take refuge in righteousness and demons in unrighteousness."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मो हि श्रूयते पक्षः सुराणाम् च महात्मनाम् || ६-३५-१३

अधर्मो रक्षसाम् पक्षोह्य असुराणाम् च रावण |
verses.translation:
"O, Ravana! Righteous is said to be on the side of the high-souled celestials.
Unrighteousness is indeed on the side of ogres and demons."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मो वै ग्रसते अधर्मम् ततः कृतम् अभूद् युगम् || ६-३५-१४

अधर्मो ग्रसते धर्मम् ततस् तिष्यः प्रवर्तते |
verses.wordMeaning:
14. yadaa = when; dharmaH = rightoeusness; grastae
= swallows; adharmam- unrighteousness; abhuut = it becomes; kR^itam yugam = Kruta
Yuga a golden age; adharmaH = (when) unrigheousness; grasate\ swallows; dharmam =
righteousness; tadaa = then; pravartate = it stimulates; tiSyaH = Kali
Yuga the fourth age.
verses.translation:
"When righteousness swallows unrighteousness, it becomes kR^ita Yuga, a golden age.
When unrighteousness swallows righteousness, it incites Kali Yuga the fourth age."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् त्वया चरता लोकान् धर्मो विनिहतो महान् || ६-३५-१५

अधर्मः प्रगृहीतसः च तेन अस्मद् बलिनः परे |
verses.translation:
"Therefore, while you were wandering in the worlds, even the great righteousness was
destroyed and unrighteousness favoured by you. So, the enemies are stronger than us."

verses.sanskrit:
स प्रमादाद् विवृद्धस् ते अधर्मो अहिर् ग्रसते हि नः || ६-३५-१६

विवर्धयति पक्षम् च सुराणाम् सुर भावनः |
verses.translation:
"Due to your negligence, that well nourished unrighteousness is swallowing us. Being
favourable to demons, the sect of demons is thereby getting augmented."

verses.sanskrit:
विषयेषु प्रसक्तेन यत् किंचित् कारिणा त्वया || ६-३५-१७

ऱ्षीणाम् अग्नि कल्पानाम् उद्वेगो जनितो महान् |
verses.wordMeaning:
17. mahaan = A great; udvegaH = fear; janitaH = has
been generated; R^iSiNaam = in the sages; agnikalpaanaam = having the nature of
fire; prasaktena = who are interested; viSayeSu = in sensual enjoyments;
yatkinchitkaariNaa = doing whatever you like.
verses.translation:
"You, who are interested in sensual enjoyments, doing whatever you like, have
created a great alarm in the sages, having the nature of fire."

verses.sanskrit:
तेषाम् प्रभावो दुर्धर्षः प्रदीप्त;इव पावकः || ६-३५-१८

तपसा भावित आत्मानो धर्मस्य अनुग्रहे रताः |

मुख्यैर् यज्नैर् यजन्त्य् एते नित्यम् तैस् तैर् द्विजातयः || ६-३५-१९

जुह्वत्य् अग्नींसः च विधिवद् वेदांसः च उच्चैर् अधीयते |

अभिभूय च रक्षांसि ब्रह्म घोषान् उदैरयन् || ६-३५-२०

दिशो विप्रद्रुताः सर्वे स्तनयित्नुर् इव उष्णगे |
verses.translation:
"The power of those sages is insurmountable like an igniting fire. Having purified
their minds through penance, they are intent on promotion of righteousness, in as much as these
Brahamans worship the gods through different principal sacrifices, also pour oblations into the
sacred fires with due ceremony and read the Vedas (Sacred texts) in a loud voice. Having subdued
the demons, they continued to chant the sacred text, on hearing which all the demons scattered in
all directions, as thundering clouds in a hot season."

verses.sanskrit:
ऋषीणाम् अग्नि कल्पानाम् अग्नि होत्र समुत्थितः || ६-३५-२१

आदत्ते रक्षसाम् तेजो धूमो व्याप्य दिशो दश |
verses.wordMeaning:
21. dhuumaH = The smoke; agnihotra samutthitaH = coming
forth from the sacred fire; R^iSiiNaam = of the sages; agnikalpaanaam = who
resemble the fire ( in brilliance); vyaapya = enveloping; dasha- the ten; dishaH
= directions; aadatte = takes away; tejaH = the magical power; rakSasaam
= of the demons.
verses.translation:
"The smoke coming forth from the sacred fire of the sage who resemble the fire (in
brilliance), enveloping the ten directions, takes away the magical power of the demons."

verses.sanskrit:
तेषु तेषु च देशेषु पुण्येषु च दृढ व्रतैः || ६-३५-२२

चर्यमाणम् तपस् तीव्रम् सम्तापयति राक्षसान् |
verses.wordMeaning:
22. tiivram = the severe; tapaH = austerity;
charyamaaNaam = practised; puNyeSTyavadhR^ita vrataiH = by the sages; firm of
resolve; teSu teSu = in different; desheSu = countries; samtaapayati =
torment; raakSasaan = the demons.
verses.translation:
"The severe austerity practised by the sages, firm of resolve, in different
countries torment the demons."

verses.sanskrit:
देवदानवयक्षेभो गृहीतश्च वरस्त्वया || ६-३५-२३

मनुष्या वानरा ऋक्षा गोलाङ्गूला महाबलाः |

बलवन्त इहागम्य गर्जन्ति दृढविक्रमाः ||६-३५-२४
verses.translation:
"You received a boon of invincibility from celestials, demons and semi-divine
beings, but then are men, bears as well as powerful and very efficacious having strong prowess who
are coming hither, roaring like lions."

verses.sanskrit:
उत्पातान् विविधान् दृष्ट्वा घोरान् बहु विधांस् तथा |

विनाशम् अनुपश्यामि सर्वेषाम् रक्षसाम् अहम् || ६-३५-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. dR^iSTvaa = by seeing; vividhaan = various kinds;
utpaataan = of unexpected events (portents); tathaa = and; bahuvidhaan =
many types; ghoraan = of terrible things; aham = I; anupashyaami = am
perceiving; vinaasham = destruction; sarveSaam = of all; rakSasaam = the
demons.
verses.translation:
"By seeing various kinds of unexpected events (portents) and many types of terrific
things, I am perceiving a destruction of all the demons."

verses.sanskrit:
खराभिस् तनिता घोरा मेघाह् प्रतिभयम् करः |

शोणितेन अभिवर्षन्ति लन्काम् उष्णेन सर्वतः || ६-३५-२६
verses.translation:
"With terrifying clamour, monstrous clouds, inspiring horror, rain hot blood on
Lanka on every side."

verses.sanskrit:
रुदताम् वाहनानाम् च प्रपतन्त्य् अस्र बिन्दवः |

ध्वजा ध्वस्ता विवर्णासः च न प्रभान्ति यथा पुरम् || ६-३५-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. ashrubindavaH = drops of tears; prapatanti = fall;
rudataam = from weeping; vaahanaanaam = elephnts; horses etc.; vivarNaaH
= which became discoloured; rajodhvastaaH = due to covering by dust; na
prabhaanti = and are not shining; yathaapuram = as before.
verses.translation:
"Drops of tears drop from the weeping elephants, horses etc., whose skins became
discoloured, covered with dust and are not shining as before."

verses.sanskrit:
व्याला गोमायवो ग्ऱ्^ इध्रा वाशन्ति च सुभैरवम् |

प्रविश्य लन्काम् अनिशम् समवायांसः च कुर्वते || ६-३५-२८
verses.translation:
"Flesh-eating animals, jackals and eagles and howling horribly. Entering Lanka, they
are in the groves, forming into groups."

verses.sanskrit:
कालिकाः पाण्डुरैर् दन्तैः प्रहसन्त्य् अग्रतः स्थिताः |

स्त्रियः स्वप्नेषु मुष्णन्त्यो गृहाणि प्रतिभाष्य च || ६-३५-२९
verses.translation:
"Black women, chattering incoherently in dreams and robbing different houses stand
in front, laughing loudly with their white teeth."

verses.sanskrit:
गृहाणाम् बलि कर्माणि श्वानः पर्युपभुन्जते |

खरा गोषु प्रजायन्ते मूषिका नकुलैः सह || ६-३५-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. shvaanaH = dogs; paryupabhuN^jate = devour;
balikarmaaNi = the sacred offerings; gR^ihaaNi = in homes; kharaaH =
donkeys; prajaayante = are born; goSu = of cows; muuSakaaH cha = and
rats; nakuleSu = of mongoose.
verses.translation:
"Dogs devour the sacred offerings offered in homes. Donkeys are born of cows and
rats of mongoose."

verses.sanskrit:
मार्जारा द्वीपिभिः सार्धम् सूकराः शुनकैः सह |

किम्नरा राक्षसैसः च अपि समेयुर् मानुषैः सह || ६-३५-३१
verses.translation:
"Cats mate with tigers, pigs with dogs, Kinnaras (a species of demi-gods with the
human figure and the head of a horse or with a horse's body and the head of a man) with demons and
men."

verses.sanskrit:
पाण्डुरा रक्त पादासः च विहगाः काल चोदिताः |

राक्षसानाम् विनाशाय कपोता विचरन्ति च || ६-३५-३२
verses.translation:
"Red-footed and white pigeons, messengers of death, move in different directions,
foretelling the extermination of demons."

verses.sanskrit:
वीचीकूचीति वाशन्त्यः शारिका वेश्मसु स्थिताः |

पतन्ति ग्रथितासः च अपि निर्जिताः कलह एषिणः || ६-३५-३३
verses.translation:
"Domesticated minas (a kind of birds) making a chirping sound, defeated by other
bellicose birds drop down, being twined together in groups."

verses.sanskrit:
पक्षिणश्च मृगाः सर्वे प्रत्यादित्यम् रुदन्ति ते |

करालो विकटो मुण्डः पुरुषः कृष्ण पिन्गलः || ६-३५-३४

कालो गृहाणि सर्वेषाम् काले काले अन्ववेक्षते |

एतान्य् अन्यानि दुष्टानि निमित्तान्य् उत्पतन्ति च || ६-३५-३५
verses.translation:
"Birds and wild animals, facing towards the sun, cry out. Death, in the form of a
frightful, monstrous and cruel blackish fellow with a shaven head casts his eyes on all our
dwellings, both morning and evening. These and such other sinister omens appear."

verses.sanskrit:
विष्णुम् मन्यामहे रामम् मानुषम् देहम् आस्थितम् |

न हि मानुष मात्रो असौ राघवो दृढ विक्रमः || ६-३५-३६

येन बद्धः समुद्रस्य स सेतुः परम अद्भुतः |

कुरुष्व नर राजेन संधिम् रामेण रावण || ६-३५-३७

ज्ञात्वावधार्य कर्माणि क्रियतामायतिक्षमम् |
verses.translation:
"I deem Rama of firm fortitude as Vishnu dwelling in human form. This Rama is not a
mere human being, he by whom that most wonderful bridge was built across the sea. O, Ravana!
Conclude peace with Rama, who is the king of men. Having come to know of his acts, let that which
is good for the future be done after a mature understanding."

verses.sanskrit:
इदम् वचस् तत्र निगद्य माल्यवन् |

परीक्ष्य रक्षो अधिपतेर् मनः पुनः |

अनुत्तमेषु उत्तम पौरुषो बली |

बभूव तूष्णीम् समवेक्ष्य रावणम् || ६-३५-३८

sargas.title: Sarga 36
sargas.introduction:
Ravana turns his deaf ear to the words of Malyavan and even abuses him that the latter 
exaggerating the strength of Rama. Ravana further says that he will not restore Seetha to Rama and that 
Rama and his army will not go back alive, having crossed the ocean and reached Lanka. Then, Ravana assigns 
Prahasta and others to guard the four main gates of Lanka against the inroads of the enemy. He retires 
to his inner apartments, after ordering these assignments.

verses.sanskrit:
तत्तु माल्यवतो वाक्यम् हितम् उक्तम् दज़ आननः |

न मर्षयति दुष्ट अत्मा कालस्य वज़म् आगतः || ६-३६-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. dashaananaH = Ravana the ten-faced monster; duSTaatmaa = the 
evil-minded; aagataH = who had fallen; vasham = under the sway; kaalasya 
= of Death; na marSayati = could not brook; tat = that; hitam = beneficial;
vaakyam = advice; uktam = uttered; maalyavataH = by Malyavan.

verses.sanskrit:
स बद्ध्वा भ्रु कुटिम् वक्त्रे क्रोधस्य वज़म् आगतः |

अमर्षात् परिवृत्त अक्षो माल्यवन्तम् अथ अब्रवीत् || ६-३६-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. saH = He; aagataH = had fallen; vasham = under the 
sway; krodhasya = of anger; baddhavaa = knitting; bhrukuTim = his brows;
vaktre = on the face; parivR^ittaakSaH = rolling his eyes; dmarSaat = in 
fury; abraviit = spoke; atha = then; maalyavantam = to Malyavan; (as follows)

verses.sanskrit:
हित बुद्ध्या यद् अहितम् वचह् परुषम् उच्यते |

पर पक्षम् प्रविज़्य एव न एतत् ज़्रोत्र गतम् मम || ६-३६-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. etat = these; ahitam = improper; paruSam = and harsh;
vachaH = words; yat = which; uchyate = were spoken (by you); hita buddhyaa 
= taking them to be salutary; pravishyaiva = and even entering into parapakSam = the side of 
an enemy; na shrotagatam = has not caught the ears; mama = to me.
verses.translation:
"I have closed my ears to the speech you have made, albeit with good intentions and even 
by taking sides with the enemy."

verses.sanskrit:
मानुषम् कृपणम् रामम् एकम् ज़ाखा मृग आज़्रयम् |

समर्थम् मन्यसे केन त्यक्तम् पित्रा वन आलयम् || ६-३६-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. kena = how; manyase = are you thinking; raamaH = 
Rama; maanuSam = who is a human being; kR^ipaNam = a feeble man; ekam = a 
small man; shaakhamR^igaashrayam = taking help from monkeys; tyaktam = abandoned;
pitraa = by his father; vanaashrayam = and dwelling in a forest; samartham 
= as competent?
verses.translation:
"How can you hold Rama, who is a mere human being, a small feeble man, taking help from 
monkeys and dwelling in a forest having been abandoned by his father, to be a competent person?"

verses.sanskrit:
रक्षसाम् ईज़्वरम् माम् च देवतानाम् भयम् करम् |

हीनम् माम् मन्यसे केन;अहीनम् सर्व विक्रमैः || ६-३६-५
verses.translation:
"How can you hold me, the Lord of demons, terrifying celestials and possessing all powers 
in full, to be a weaker person?"

verses.sanskrit:
वीर द्वेषेण वा ज़न्के पक्ष पातेन वा रिपोः |

त्वया अहम् परुषाण्य् उक्तः पर प्रोत्साहनेन वा || ६-३६-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. aham = I; shaN^ke = suspect; paruSaaNi = (that) these 
harsh words; uktaH = were spoken; tvayaa = by you; viiradveSeNa = because 
of your hero-hatred; pakSapatena vaa = or your partiality; ripoH = towards the enemies;
protsahanena vaa = or my (wrong) encouragement.
verses.translation:
"I suspect that you spoke these harsh words to me because of your envy of my prowess 
or your partiality towards the enemies or my lenience towards you."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रभवन्तम् पदस्थम् हि परुषम् को अह्बिधास्यति |

पण्डितह् ज़ास्त्र तत्त्वज्नो विना प्रोत्साहनाद् रिपोः || ६-३६-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. kaH = which; panditaH = learned man; shaastra tattvajJNaH 
= understanding the truth in the scriptures abhibhaashhate = would speak; paruSam = thus harshly;
padastham = to a person in power; prabhavantam = who is mighty vinaa protsaahanena 
vaa = without any instigation?
verses.translation:
"Which learned man, understand the truth in the scriptures, would speak thus harshly 
to a mighty person in power, were it not to instigate him?"

verses.sanskrit:
आनीय च वनात् सीताम् पद्म हीनाम् इव ज़्रियम् |

किम् अर्थम् प्रतिदास्यामि राघवस्य भयाद् अहम् || ६-३६-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. kimartham = why; aham = (should) I; pratidaasyaam 
= give back; siitaam = Seetha; shriyam iva = who is like Lashmi; the goddess of fortune;
padmahiinaam = without the loutus; aaniiya = having brought her; vanaat = 
from the forest; bhayaat = through fear; raaghavasya of Rama?
verses.translation:
"Why should I give back Seetha, who is like Lakshmi the goddess of fortune without the 
lotus, having brought her away from the forest, through fear of Rama?"

verses.sanskrit:
वृतम् वानर कोटीभिः ससुग्रीवम् सलक्ष्मणम् |

पज़्य कैज़्चिद् अहोभिस् त्वम् राघवम् निहतम् मया || ६-३६-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. kaishchit = within a few; ahobhiH = days; pashya 
= see; raaghvam = Rama; salakSmaNam = along with Lakshama; sasngriivam =
